---
title: "Georgia_Ticks_Biodiv"
author: "Ian Pshea-Smith"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 1. INSTALL REQUIRED PACKAGES | RUN ONLY IF PACKAGES NEED INSTALLATION
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r PACKAGE INSTALLATION}

  if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
    pacman::p_load(readxl, dplyr, purrr, stringr, lubridate, 
                   ggplot2, sf, rnaturalearth, MASS, raster, 
                   geodata, modisfast, parallel, exactextractr, 
                   dismo, viridis, htmltools, gbm, patchwork,
                   geodist, iNEXT, cooccur, igraph, visNetwork,
                   ClustGeo, vegan, cluster, fpc, indicspecies,
                   classInt, corrplot, reshape2)

```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 2. DATA ACQUISITION AND INITIAL PROCESSING
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r DATA ACQUISITION AND INITIAL PROCESSING}

  # Import tick occurrence data from GitHub repository
      url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IanPsheaSmith/GeorgiaTickDist/main/TicksEurope_Clean.xlsx"
      
      # Download to temporary file
        temp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx")
        download.file(url, temp_file, mode = "wb")
      
      # Import data from sheet 2
        Ticks_Raw <- read_excel(temp_file, sheet = 2)
  
  # Data cleaning procedures
      # Remove duplicate observations while preserving unique sample identifiers
        Ticks_Raw <- Ticks_Raw %>%
            distinct(across(-c(Sample_ID, Collection_ID)), .keep_all = TRUE)
      
      # Remove observations without spatial coordinates
        Ticks_Raw <- Ticks_Raw %>% 
            filter(!is.na(Latitude) & !is.na(Longitude))
      
      # Standardize date column names and format
        Ticks_Raw <- Ticks_Raw %>%
            rename(Start_Date = `Start_ Date`) %>%
            mutate(Start_Date = as.Date(Start_Date),
                   End_Date = as.Date(End_Date))
  
  # Separate data by country of collection
      # Identify unique countries in dataset
       unique_countries <- unique(Ticks_Raw$Country)
      
      # Split dataset by country
        ticks_list <- split(Ticks_Raw, Ticks_Raw$Country)
      
      # Create separate dataframes for each country
        list2env(setNames(ticks_list, paste0("Ticks_Raw_", names(ticks_list))), 
                 envir = .GlobalEnv)
  
  # Convert Georgia data to spatial format
      # Create spatial points from coordinate data (WGS84 projection)
        Ticks_Georgia <- Ticks_Raw_Georgia %>%
            st_as_sf(coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326)
      
      # Import Georgia administrative boundary
        Georgia_Boundary <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", country = "Georgia", 
                                        returnclass = "sf")
        
      # Limit analysis to points within national boundary
        Ticks_Georgia <- st_intersection(Ticks_Georgia, Georgia_Boundary)
      
      # Visualize sampling locations
        ggplot() +
            geom_sf(data = Georgia_Boundary, fill = "white", color = "black") +
            geom_sf(data = Ticks_Georgia, color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
            theme_minimal() +
            labs(title = "Tick Collection Sites in Georgia")
```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 3. SPECIES-SPECIFIC DATASET CREATION
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r SPECIES-SPECIFIC DATASET CREATION}

  # Define focal tick species for analysis
      species_list <- c(
          "Ixodes ricinus", "Dermacentor reticulatus", "Dermacentor marginatus",
          "Haemaphysalis parva", "Haemaphysalis punctata", "Haemaphysalis inermis",
          "Rhipicephalus bursa", "Rhipicephalus annulatus", "Rhipicephalus rossicus",
          "Rhipicephalus turanicus", "Hyalomma marginatum", "Haemaphysalis sulcata",
          "Hyalomma aegyptium", "Rhipicephalus sanguineus", "Haemaphysalis concinna",
          "Haemaphysalis caucasica", "Haemaphysalis erinacei", "Hyalomma impeltatum",
          "Ixodes acuminatus", "Rhipicephalus pusillus", "Hyalomma scupense"
      )
  
  # Create standardized species identifiers for file naming
      clean_names <- setNames(gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]+", "_", species_list), species_list)
  
  # Generate spatial presence/absence datasets for each species
      spatial_species_PA_datasets <- map(
          setNames(species_list, clean_names),
          function(species) {
              # Extract genus and species components
              name_parts <- strsplit(species, " ")[[1]]
              genus <- name_parts[1]
              specific_epithet <- name_parts[2]
              
              # Determine presence/absence at each sampling location
              Ticks_Georgia %>%
                  group_by(geometry) %>%
                  summarise(
                      # Record presence (1) if species was found at this location
                      Presence = as.integer(any(Genus == genus & Species == specific_epithet)),
                      .groups = "drop"
                  ) %>%
                  dplyr::select(Presence, geometry)
          }
      )
  
  # Construct a combined multi-species dataset
      # Initialize with first species
        first_species <- names(spatial_species_PA_datasets)[1]
        combined_spatial_data <- spatial_species_PA_datasets[[first_species]] %>%
            rename(!!first_species := Presence)
      
      # Add remaining species sequentially
        remaining_species <- names(spatial_species_PA_datasets)[-1]
        combined_spatial_data <- reduce(
            remaining_species,
            function(combined_df, species_name) {
                # Extract presence values for current species
                  presence_values <- spatial_species_PA_datasets[[species_name]] %>%
                      st_drop_geometry() %>%
                      dplyr::pull(Presence)
                
                # Add as new column to combined dataset
                  combined_df %>% 
                      mutate(!!species_name := presence_values)
            },
            .init = combined_spatial_data
        )
  
  # Calculate prevalence statistics for each species
      species_summary <- map_df(
          names(spatial_species_PA_datasets),
          function(species_name) {
              data <- spatial_species_PA_datasets[[species_name]]
              presence_count <- sum(data$Presence)
              total_count <- nrow(data)
              
              tibble(
                  Species = names(which(clean_names == species_name)),
                  Presences = presence_count,
                  Total = total_count,
                  Percentage = round(100 * presence_count / total_count, 1)
              )
          }
      )
      
      # Display species prevalence data
        print(species_summary)
  
  # Generate species-specific presence-only datasets for modeling
      presence_only_datasets <- map(
          names(spatial_species_PA_datasets),
          function(species_name) {
              # Filter to presence records only
                spatial_species_PA_datasets[[species_name]] %>%
                    filter(Presence == 1) %>%
                  # Retain taxonomic information
                    mutate(species = names(which(clean_names == species_name)))
          }
      )
      
      # Name each dataset consistently
        names(presence_only_datasets) <- names(spatial_species_PA_datasets)
  
  # Summarize presence-only datasets
      presence_summary <- map_df(
          names(presence_only_datasets),
          function(species_name) {
              data <- presence_only_datasets[[species_name]]
              
              tibble(
                  Species = names(which(clean_names == species_name)),
                  Presence_Points = nrow(data)
              )
          }
      )
      
      # Display presence dataset summary
        cat("\nPresence-Only Dataset Summary:\n")
        print(presence_summary)
  
  # Export individual presence datasets to global environment for further analysis
      invisible(
          map2(
              names(presence_only_datasets),
              presence_only_datasets,
              function(name, dataset) {
                  # Create descriptive object name
                    object_name <- paste0(name, "_presence")
                  
                  # Export to global environment
                    assign(object_name, dataset, envir = .GlobalEnv)
                  
                  # Return name reference
                    object_name
              }
          )
      )
```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 4. FIGURE 1: TICK SAMPLING LOCATIONS AND DENSITY MAP FOR GEORGIA
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r FIGURE 1: TICK SAMPLING LOCATIONS AND DENSITY MAP FOR GEORGIA}

    # Prepare tick location data for kernel density estimation
        # Extract spatial coordinates for analysis
          coords <- st_coordinates(Ticks_Georgia)
        
        # Create weighted dataset based on tick count at each location
          Ticks_Georgia_Expanded <- Ticks_Georgia[rep(1:nrow(Ticks_Georgia), Ticks_Georgia$Vector.Count), ]
          coords <- st_coordinates(Ticks_Georgia_Expanded)
    
    # Define spatial extent for density estimation
        # Obtain Georgia's geographic boundaries
          georgia_bbox <- st_bbox(Georgia_Boundary)
        
        # Create density estimation grid
          kde_x_range <- seq(georgia_bbox$xmin, georgia_bbox$xmax, length.out = 1000)
          kde_y_range <- seq(georgia_bbox$ymin, georgia_bbox$ymax, length.out = 1000)
    
    # Calculate kernel density estimate
        kde <- kde2d(
            x = coords[,1], 
            y = coords[,2], 
            h = c(bandwidth.nrd(coords[,1]), bandwidth.nrd(coords[,2])),
            n = c(1000, 1000),
            lims = c(georgia_bbox$xmin, georgia_bbox$xmax, georgia_bbox$ymin, georgia_bbox$ymax)
        )
    
    # Convert kernel density to geospatial format
        # Transform to raster
          kde_raster <- raster::raster(kde)
        
        # Constrain to Georgia's administrative boundary
          kde_raster_cropped <- mask(crop(kde_raster, Georgia_Boundary), Georgia_Boundary)
        
        # Convert to dataframe for visualization
          kde_df <- as.data.frame(raster::rasterToPoints(kde_raster_cropped))
          colnames(kde_df) <- c("Longitude", "Latitude", "Density")
    
    # Create main tick distribution map
        KDEMap <- ggplot() +
            # Add Georgia boundary
              geom_sf(data = Georgia_Boundary, fill = "gray90", color = "black") +
            # Add density surface
              geom_tile(data = kde_df, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, fill = Density), alpha = 0.7) +
              scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "viridis", name = "Tick Density") +
            # Add sampling points
              geom_sf(data = Ticks_Georgia, color = "black", alpha = 1, size = 1) +
            # Format plot aesthetics
              theme_minimal() +
              labs(title = "", x = "", y = "")
    
    # Create inset map showing regional context
        # Obtain geographic data for Caucasus region
          world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")
        
        # Define countries in the Caucasus region
          caucasus_countries <- c("Georgia", "Armenia", "Azerbaijan", "Russia", "Turkey", "Iran")
          caucasus <- world[world$name %in% caucasus_countries, ]
          georgia <- world[world$name == "Georgia", ]
        
        # Create regional context map
          caucasus_map <- ggplot() +
              # Add surrounding countries
                geom_sf(data = caucasus, fill = "grey90", color = "black", size = 0.3) +
              # Highlight Georgia
                geom_sf(data = georgia, fill = "#754680", color = "black", size = 0.3) +
              # Set appropriate geographic extent
                coord_sf(
                    xlim = c(38, 50),
                    ylim = c(37, 45)
                ) +
              # Remove unnecessary elements
                theme_void() +
                theme(
                    legend.position = "none",
                    #plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0),
                    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white")
              )
    
    # Display Figure 1
        print(KDEMap)
        print(caucasus_map)
        # Inset map & Titling created using InkScape or MS PowerPoint

```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 5. FIGURE 2 & TABLE 1 | COUNTS & BIODIVERSITY SUMMARY
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r FIGURE 2 & TABLE 1}

# ==============================================================================
# STEP 1: CREATE WHITTAKER RANK-ABUNDANCE PLOT
# ==============================================================================
    
    # Generate enhanced Whittaker plot with raw count overlay
        Figure2 <- presence_summary %>%
            arrange(desc(Presence_Points)) %>%
            mutate(
                rank = row_number(),
                log_abundance = log10(Presence_Points),
                genus = str_extract(Species, "^\\w+")
            ) %>%
            ggplot(aes(x = rank)) +
                # Background bars showing raw occurrence counts
                    geom_col(
                        aes(y = Presence_Points), 
                        alpha = 0.4, 
                        fill = "#21908c", 
                        color = "#21908c",
                        width = 0.6
                    ) +
                # Whittaker curve overlay (log-transformed smoothed line)
                    geom_smooth(
                        aes(y = 10^log_abundance), 
                        method = "loess", 
                        span = 0.4,
                        color = "#218e59", 
                        size = 1.5, 
                        se = FALSE,
                        alpha = 0.8
                    ) +
                # Data points colored by genus
                    geom_point(
                        aes(y = 10^log_abundance, fill = genus), 
                        size = 4, 
                        shape = 21,
                        color = "#218e59",
                        stroke = 1.2
                    ) +
                # Color scheme and axis formatting
                    scale_fill_viridis_d(
                        option = "viridis", 
                        name = "Genus"
                    ) +
                    scale_y_continuous(
                        name = "Number of Presence Records",
                        breaks = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100, 125),
                        sec.axis = sec_axis(
                            trans = ~ log10(. + 1),
                            name = "Log(Records + 1)",
                            breaks = log10(c(1, 10, 25, 50, 100, 125) + 1),
                            labels = round(log10(c(1, 10, 25, 50, 100, 125) + 1), 1)
                        )
                    ) +
                # Labels and theme
                    labs(
                        title = "Georgia Tick Species: Raw Abundance vs. Log-Rank Pattern",
                        subtitle = "Bars show raw counts | Green curve and points show classic Whittaker relationship",
                        x = "Species Rank"
                    ) +
                    theme_minimal() +
                    theme(
                        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
                        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
                        axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "#218e59"),
                        axis.text.y.right = element_text(color = "#218e59", size = 9),
                        legend.position = "bottom"
                    )
  
# ==============================================================================
# STEP 2: CALCULATE SPECIES-LEVEL DIVERSITY COMPONENTS
# ==============================================================================
  
    # Calculate individual species contributions to diversity metrics
        species_diversity <- presence_summary %>%
            mutate(
                # Proportional abundance for each species
                    prop_abundance = Presence_Points / sum(Presence_Points),
                
                # Abundance rank (highest to lowest)
                    abundance_rank = rank(-Presence_Points, ties.method = "min"),
                
                # Shannon diversity component for each species
                # CORRECTED: Fixed variable consistency error
                    shannon_component = ifelse(prop_abundance > 0, 
                                               -prop_abundance * log(prop_abundance), 0),
                
                # Simpson dominance component for each species (original Simpson λ)
                    simpson_component = prop_abundance^2
            ) %>%
            arrange(desc(Presence_Points))
  
# ==============================================================================
# STEP 3: COMPUTE COMMUNITY-LEVEL DIVERSITY INDICES
# ==============================================================================
  
    # Calculate actual number of species present (not dataset rows)
        S <- sum(species_diversity$prop_abundance > 0)
    
    # Calculate comprehensive community diversity metrics
        community_metrics <- list(
            # Basic abundance measures
                total_abundance = sum(species_diversity$Presence_Points),
                species_richness = S,  
            
            # Shannon diversity family
                shannon_diversity = sum(species_diversity$shannon_component),
                shannon_evenness = sum(species_diversity$shannon_component) / log(S),
            
            # Simpson diversity family (using original Simpson nomenclature)
                simpson_dominance = sum(species_diversity$simpson_component),     # Original Simpson λ
                gini_simpson = 1 - sum(species_diversity$simpson_component),      # Gini-Simpson (1-λ)
                simpson_evenness = (1 / sum(species_diversity$simpson_component)) / S,
            
            # Dominance measures
                berger_parker = max(species_diversity$prop_abundance),
            
            # Hill numbers (effective species diversity)
                hill_q0 = S,                                                      # Species richness
                hill_q1 = exp(sum(species_diversity$shannon_component)),          # Shannon effective species
                hill_q2 = 1 / sum(species_diversity$simpson_component)            # Simpson effective species
        )
    
    # Display community metrics with clarified Simpson terminology
        cat("Community-level Diversity Metrics:\n")
        cat("Note: Using corrected formulations with proper Simpson nomenclature\n")
        print(community_metrics)
  
# ==============================================================================
# STEP 4: CLASSIFY SPECIES BY DOMINANCE PATTERNS
# ==============================================================================

    # Add ecological classifications to species data
        species_detailed <- species_diversity %>%
            mutate(
                # Assign dominance classes based on abundance rank
                    dominance_class = case_when(
                        abundance_rank == 1 ~ "Dominant",
                        abundance_rank <= 3 ~ "Codominant", 
                        abundance_rank <= 10 ~ "Common",
                        abundance_rank <= 15 ~ "Occasional",
                        TRUE ~ "Rare"
                    ),
                
                # Categorize by proportional abundance
                    abundance_category = case_when(
                        prop_abundance >= 0.1 ~ "Very Abundant (≥10%)",
                        prop_abundance >= 0.05 ~ "Abundant (5-10%)",
                        prop_abundance >= 0.02 ~ "Moderate (2-5%)",
                        prop_abundance >= 0.01 ~ "Low (1-2%)",
                        TRUE ~ "Very Low (<1%)"
                    ),
                
                # Calculate cumulative abundance for dominance curves
                    cumulative_abundance = cumsum(prop_abundance),
                
                # Measure deviation from perfect evenness
                    evenness_deviation = abs(prop_abundance - (1/S)),  
                
                # Extract genus for taxonomic grouping
                    genus = str_extract(Species, "^\\w+")
            ) %>%
            arrange(desc(Presence_Points))
    
    # Display species-level analysis
        cat("\nSpecies-level Diversity Analysis:\n")
        print(species_detailed %>% 
              dplyr::select(Species, Presence_Points, prop_abundance, abundance_rank, 
                     dominance_class, cumulative_abundance))

# ==============================================================================
# STEP 5: CREATE DOMINANCE AND EVENNESS VISUALIZATIONS
# ==============================================================================

    # Plot 1: Species dominance curve (Lorenz-type curve)
        p1 <- species_detailed %>%
            mutate(species_rank_prop = abundance_rank / max(abundance_rank)) %>%
            ggplot(aes(x = species_rank_prop, y = cumulative_abundance)) +
                geom_line(size = 1.2, color = "steelblue") +
                geom_point(aes(color = genus), size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
                geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", 
                           color = "red", alpha = 0.6) +
                scale_color_viridis_d(name = "Genus") +
                labs(
                    title = "Species Dominance Curve",
                    subtitle = "Deviation from red line indicates inequality",
                    x = "Proportional Species Rank",
                    y = "Cumulative Proportion of Total Abundance",
                    caption = "Perfect evenness = diagonal red line"
                ) +
                theme_minimal()
    
    # Plot 2: Abundance profile by dominance class
        p2 <- species_detailed %>%
            ggplot(aes(x = abundance_rank, y = prop_abundance)) +
                geom_col(aes(fill = dominance_class), alpha = 0.8, width = 0.7) +
                geom_hline(yintercept = 1/S,                                      
                          linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
                scale_fill_brewer(type = "qual", palette = "Set2", name = "Dominance\nClass") +
                labs(
                    title = "Species Abundance Profile",
                    subtitle = "Red line = perfect evenness threshold",
                    x = "Species Rank (by abundance)",
                    y = "Proportional Abundance",
                    caption = paste0("Perfect evenness = ", round(1/S, 3))      
                ) +
                theme_minimal()
    
    # Plot 3: Hill numbers comparison
        hill_data <- data.frame(
            q_value = c(0, 1, 2),
            diversity_order = c("Species Richness (q=0)", 
                               "Shannon Effective (q=1)", 
                               "Simpson Effective (q=2)"),
            effective_species = c(community_metrics$hill_q0,
                                 community_metrics$hill_q1,
                                 community_metrics$hill_q2)
        )
        
        p3 <- hill_data %>%
            ggplot(aes(x = factor(q_value), y = effective_species)) +
                geom_col(fill = "coral", alpha = 0.7, width = 0.6) +
                geom_text(aes(label = round(effective_species, 1)), 
                         vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
                labs(
                    title = "Hill Numbers (Effective Species Diversity)",
                    subtitle = "Different sensitivity to rare vs. common species",
                    x = "Diversity Order (q)",
                    y = "Effective Number of Species",
                    caption = "q=0: richness, q=1: typical diversity, q=2: dominance"
                ) +
                theme_minimal()

# ==============================================================================
# STEP 6: DISPLAY RESULTS AND CREATE SUMMARY TABLE
# ==============================================================================

    # Display all diversity plots
        print(p1)
        print(p2) 
        print(p3)
    
    # Create comprehensive diversity summary table with corrected terminology
        diversity_summary <- data.frame(
            Metric = c("Species Richness", "Shannon Diversity (H')", 
                       "Pielou Evenness (J')", "Simpson Dominance (λ)",        # CORRECTED: Proper nomenclature
                       "Gini-Simpson (1-λ)", "Simpson Evenness",               # CORRECTED: Clarified naming
                       "Berger-Parker Dominance", "Hill q=1", "Hill q=2"),
            Value = c(community_metrics$species_richness,
                      round(community_metrics$shannon_diversity, 3),
                      round(community_metrics$shannon_evenness, 3),
                      round(community_metrics$simpson_dominance, 3),
                      round(community_metrics$gini_simpson, 3),                 # CORRECTED: Use Gini-Simpson
                      round(community_metrics$simpson_evenness, 3),
                      round(community_metrics$berger_parker, 3),
                      round(community_metrics$hill_q1, 1),
                      round(community_metrics$hill_q2, 1)),
            Interpretation = c("Total number of species",
                              "Higher = more diverse",
                              "0-1 scale, 1 = perfectly even",
                              "0-1 scale, higher = more dominated (original Simpson)",
                              "0-1 scale, higher = more diverse",               # CORRECTED: Proper interpretation
                              "Effective species / total species", 
                              "Proportion of most abundant species",
                              "Effective species (Shannon-based)",
                              "Effective species (Simpson-based)")
        )
        
        cat("\nTick Community Diversity Summary:\n")
        cat("CORRECTED VERSION - Fixed variable consistency and Simpson nomenclature\n")
        print(diversity_summary)

# ==============================================================================
# STEP 7: VALIDATION AND QUALITY CHECKS
# ==============================================================================

    # Add validation checks for corrected calculations
        cat("\n=== VALIDATION CHECKS ===\n")
        cat("1. Species count consistency:", S, "species\n")
        cat("2. Proportional abundances sum to:", round(sum(species_diversity$prop_abundance), 6), "(should = 1.0)\n")
        cat("3. Simpson components sum to:", round(sum(species_diversity$simpson_component), 6), "\n")
        cat("4. Hill q0 equals species richness:", community_metrics$hill_q0 == S, "\n")
        cat("5. Hill q1 range check (should be ≤ S):", community_metrics$hill_q1 <= S, "\n")
        cat("6. Hill q2 range check (should be ≤ S):", community_metrics$hill_q2 <= S, "\n")


```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 6. IMPORTING RASTER DATA | MAY REQUIRE LOCAL STORAGE SPACE...
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r IMPORTING RASTER DATA}

  # Define local storage location for raster datasets
      local_folder <- "Georgia_Tick_Rasters"
      dir.create(local_folder, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  
  # Define source repository information
      base_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IanPsheaSmith/GeorgiaTickDist/main/CovariateRasters/"
  
  # Define list of environmental rasters to acquire
      raster_files <- c(
          # Bioclimatic variables
            "Annual_Mean_Temperature.tif", 
            "Annual_Precipitation.tif",
            "Isothermality.tif",
            "Max_Temperature_Warmest_Month.tif", 
            "Mean_Diurnal_Range.tif",
            "Mean_Temperature_Coldest_Quarter.tif", 
            "Mean_Temperature_Driest_Quarter.tif",
            "Mean_Temperature_Warmest_Quarter.tif", 
            "Mean_Temperature_Wettest_Quarter.tif",
            "Min_Temperature_Coldest_Month.tif",
            "Precipitation_Coldest_Quarter.tif", 
            "Precipitation_Driest_Month.tif",
            "Precipitation_Driest_Quarter.tif", 
            "Precipitation_Seasonality.tif",
            "Precipitation_Warmest_Quarter.tif", 
            "Precipitation_Wettest_Month.tif",
            "Precipitation_Wettest_Quarter.tif",
            "Temperature_Annual_Range.tif",
            "Temperature_Seasonality.tif",
          
          # Soil properties
            "BulkDensity.tif",
            "CoarseFragments.tif",
            "Nitrogen.tif",
            "SandContent.tif", 
            "SiltContent.tif",
            "SoilOrganicCarbon.tif", 
            "SoilpH.tif",
          
          # Topographic variables
            "Elevation.tif", 
            "Slope.tif",
          
          # Vegetation indices
            "NDVI_mean.tif", 
            "EVI_mean.tif",
          
          # Landscape metrics
            "landscape_diversity_shdi.tif", 
            "landcover_class.tif"
      )
  
  # Download raster files from repository
      cat("Downloading environmental raster files...\n")
      
      raster_files %>%
          walk(function(file) {
              cat("Downloading:", file, "... ")
              download.file(
                  url = paste0(base_url, file),
                  destfile = file.path(local_folder, file),
                  mode = "wb"  # Binary mode for raster files
              )
              cat("Done\n")
          })
  
  # Load downloaded files into a multi-layer raster stack
      CovStack <- stack(list.files(local_folder, pattern = "\\.tif$", full.names = TRUE))
      
      cat("Loaded raster stack with", nlayers(CovStack), "environmental layers\n")
      
      # Display basic information about the covariate stack
      cat("\nEnvironmental covariate information:\n")
      cat("Dimensions:", nrow(CovStack), "rows,", ncol(CovStack), "cols,", ncell(CovStack), "cells\n")
      cat("Resolution:", res(CovStack)[1], "x", res(CovStack)[2], "degrees\n")
      cat("Extent:", extent(CovStack)[1], "to", extent(CovStack)[2], "E,", 
          extent(CovStack)[3], "to", extent(CovStack)[4], "N\n")
      cat("Coordinate system:", crs(CovStack, asText = TRUE), "\n")

```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 7. EXTRACTING RASTER DATA TO PRES POINTS FOR TRUE PRESENCE/ABSENCE DATA
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r EXTRACTING RASTER DATA}

  # Define function to calculate landscape diversity (Shannon's Diversity Index)
      calculate_shdi <- function(values) {
          # Remove NA values
            values <- values[!is.na(values)]
          
          # If no valid values, return NA
            if (length(values) == 0) {
                return(NA_real_)
            }
          
          # Count occurrences of each unique value
            value_counts <- table(values)
          
          # Calculate proportions
            proportions <- value_counts / sum(value_counts)
            
          # Calculate Shannon's Diversity Index
            shdi <- -sum(proportions * log(proportions))
          
          return(shdi)
      }
  
  # Create standardized species code system for dataset naming
      create_species_abbreviation <- function(species) {
          parts <- strsplit(species, "_")[[1]]
          genus_abbr <- substr(parts[1], 1, 2)
          species_abbr <- substr(parts[2], 1, 3)
          paste0(genus_abbr, species_abbr)
      }
      
      # Generate abbreviation mapping
        all_species <- names(spatial_species_PA_datasets)
        species_abbr <- map_chr(all_species, create_species_abbreviation)
        name_mapping <- setNames(species_abbr, all_species)
  
  # Ensure environmental data is in appropriate format
      if (inherits(CovStack, "SpatRaster")) {
          cov_raster <- stack(CovStack)
      } else {
          cov_raster <- CovStack
      }
      
      # Extract landcover layer for landscape diversity calculations
        landcover_raster <- cov_raster$landcover_class
  
  # Extract spatial point geometries
      first_species <- spatial_species_PA_datasets[[1]]
      all_point_geometries <- first_species
      n_points <- nrow(all_point_geometries)
  
  # Define batch processing strategy for memory efficiency
      batch_size <- 20
      batch_indices <- split(1:n_points, ceiling(1:n_points / batch_size))
  
  # Extract environmental covariates for all points
      all_covariates <- batch_indices %>%
          imap_dfr(function(indices, batch_num) {
              # Process each point in the batch
                batch_results <- indices %>%
                    map_dfr(function(i) {
                      # Get the point
                        point <- all_point_geometries[i,]
                      
                      # Extract covariates at point location
                        covariates <- raster::extract(cov_raster, point)
                      
                      # Create buffer for landscape diversity calculation
                        point_utm <- st_transform(point, crs = 32638)
                        buffer_utm <- st_buffer(point_utm, dist = 1000)
                        buffer_wgs84 <- st_transform(buffer_utm, crs = st_crs(landcover_raster))
                      
                      # Extract landcover values within buffer
                        landcover_values <- raster::extract(landcover_raster, buffer_wgs84)
                        landcover_values <- unlist(landcover_values)
                      
                      # Calculate landscape diversity
                        shdi <- calculate_shdi(landcover_values)
                      
                      # Create result data frame for this point
                        point_result <- as.data.frame(covariates)
                        point_result$landscape_diversity_shdi <- shdi
                        point_result$point_id <- i
                      
                      return(point_result)
                  })
              
              # Release memory
                gc(verbose = FALSE)
              
              return(batch_results)
          })
  
  # Format and augment covariate data
      # Convert landcover to factor variable
        if ("landcover_class" %in% names(all_covariates)) {
            all_covariates$landcover_class <- as.factor(all_covariates$landcover_class)
        }
      
      # Add geographic coordinates
        coords <- st_coordinates(all_point_geometries)
        all_covariates$Longitude <- coords[, "X"]
        all_covariates$Latitude <- coords[, "Y"]
  
  # Data quality assessment
      na_counts <- colSums(is.na(all_covariates))
      cols_with_na <- names(which(na_counts > 0))
      
  # Create species-specific BRT datasets
      # Function to process individual species
        process_species_presence <- function(species_name) {
          # Get species-specific presence/absence data
            species_data <- spatial_species_PA_datasets[[species_name]]
          
          # Add point ID for joining
            species_data$point_id <- 1:nrow(species_data)
          
          # Extract presence column
            presence_data <- data.frame(
                point_id = species_data$point_id,
                Presence = species_data$Presence
            )
          
          # Join with covariates
            species_dataset <- left_join(all_covariates, presence_data, by = "point_id")
          
          # Drop point_id column
            species_dataset <- dplyr::select(species_dataset, -point_id)
          
          # Calculate presence statistics
            presence_count <- sum(species_dataset$Presence)
            presence_pct <- round(100 * presence_count / nrow(species_dataset), 1)
          
          # Create object in global environment
            object_name <- paste0(name_mapping[species_name], "_BRTData")
            assign(object_name, species_dataset, envir = .GlobalEnv)
          
          # Return dataset and metadata
            list(
                dataset = species_dataset,
                name = species_name,
                object_name = object_name,
                presence_count = presence_count,
                presence_pct = presence_pct
            )
      }
      
      # Process all species
        species_BRT_datasets <- map(all_species, process_species_presence)
        names(species_BRT_datasets) <- all_species
        
      # Extract datasets from list structure
        species_BRT_datasets_simple <- map(species_BRT_datasets, ~ .$dataset)
        names(species_BRT_datasets_simple) <- all_species
  
  # Generate summary statistics
      summary_df <- map_dfr(species_BRT_datasets, function(result) {
          tibble(
              Species = gsub("_", " ", result$name),
              Dataset = result$object_name,
              Rows = nrow(result$dataset),
              Columns = ncol(result$dataset),
              Presence = result$presence_count,
              Presence_Pct = paste0(result$presence_pct, "%"),
              SHDI_Mean = round(mean(result$dataset$landscape_diversity_shdi, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
          )
      })

```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 8. PSEUDO-ABSENCE POINT GENERATION
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r Creating Pseudo-Absence Datasets}

  # Define function to create standardized species codes
      create_species_abbreviation <- function(species) {
          parts <- strsplit(species, "_")[[1]]
          genus_abbr <- substr(parts[1], 1, 2)
          species_abbr <- substr(parts[2], 1, 3)
          paste0(genus_abbr, species_abbr)
      }
  
  # Identify species with sufficient presence records for modeling
      species_with_sufficient_presence <- c(
          "Dermacentor_marginatus", 
          "Dermacentor_reticulatus",
          "Haemaphysalis_inermis",
          "Haemaphysalis_parva",
          "Haemaphysalis_punctata",
          "Haemaphysalis_sulcata",
          "Hyalomma_marginatum",
          "Ixodes_ricinus",
          "Rhipicephalus_annulatus",
          "Rhipicephalus_bursa",
          "Rhipicephalus_rossicus",
          "Rhipicephalus_turanicus"
      )
      
      # Create abbreviation mapping for dataset naming
        species_abbr <- map_chr(species_with_sufficient_presence, create_species_abbreviation)
        name_mapping <- setNames(species_abbr, species_with_sufficient_presence)
  
  # Define utility functions for spatial operations
      # Convert coordinate data to spatial features
        create_sf_points <- function(coords, crs = 4326) {
            sf::st_as_sf(coords, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = crs)
        }
      
      # Generate spatially-stratified pseudo-absence points
        generate_pseudo_absences <- function(species_dataset, min_dist = 25000, crs = 4326, seed_val = 123) {
          # Set seed for reproducibility
            set.seed(seed_val)
          
          # Extract presence points with coordinates
            presence_points <- species_dataset %>%
                filter(Presence == 1) %>%
                dplyr::select(Longitude, Latitude)
            
            n_presence <- nrow(presence_points)
            
          # Create sf object from presence points
            presence_sf <- create_sf_points(presence_points)
          
          # Create a buffer around presence points (25km)
            presence_buffer <- st_buffer(
                st_transform(presence_sf, crs = 32638), 
                dist = min_dist
            ) %>%
                st_transform(crs = crs)
            
          # Union all buffers
            exclusion_zone <- st_union(presence_buffer)
          
          # Get area within Georgia excluding the buffer zones
            valid_area <- st_difference(Georgia_Boundary, exclusion_zone)
          
          # Generate random points within the valid area
            pseudo_absences_sf <- st_sample(valid_area, size = n_presence) %>%
                st_sf() %>%
                mutate(Presence = 0)
            
          # Convert to data frame with coordinates
            pseudo_absences <- cbind(
                st_coordinates(pseudo_absences_sf) %>% 
                    as.data.frame() %>%
                    rename(Longitude = X, Latitude = Y),
                Presence = 0
            )
          
          return(pseudo_absences)
      }
  
  # Generate multiple replicate sets of pseudo-absence points
      n_replicates <- 10
      seed_values <- 1001:(1000 + n_replicates)
      
      # Create nested structure: species -> replicate -> points
        pseudo_absence_sets <- map(species_with_sufficient_presence, function(species) {
          # Retrieve the original species dataset
            dataset_abbr <- name_mapping[species]
            dataset_name <- paste0(dataset_abbr, "_BRTData")
            original_dataset <- get(dataset_name)
          
          # Extract presence points
            presence_points <- original_dataset %>%
                filter(Presence == 1) %>%
                dplyr::select(Longitude, Latitude, Presence)
            
          # Create replicate sets of pseudo-absence points
            replicate_sets <- map(1:n_replicates, function(rep_num) {
              # Generate pseudo-absence points with specific seed
                pseudo_absences <- generate_pseudo_absences(
                    original_dataset, 
                    seed_val = seed_values[rep_num]
                )
              
              # Return point sets and metadata
                list(
                    presence = presence_points,
                    pseudo_absence = pseudo_absences,
                    combined = bind_rows(presence_points, pseudo_absences),
                    replicate = rep_num,
                    seed = seed_values[rep_num]
                )
          })
          
          # Name the replicates
            names(replicate_sets) <- paste0("replicate_", 1:n_replicates)
          
          return(replicate_sets)
      })
      
      # Name the list elements
        names(pseudo_absence_sets) <- species_with_sufficient_presence
  
  # Visualize presence and pseudo-absence distribution
      species_maps <- map(species_with_sufficient_presence, function(species) {
          # Get points from first replicate
            points <- pseudo_absence_sets[[species]]$replicate_1
          
          # Create sf objects
            points_sf <- create_sf_points(points$combined)
          
          # Format species name for display
            display_name <- gsub("_", " ", species)
          
          # Create visualization
            ggplot() +
                geom_sf(data = Georgia_Boundary, fill = NA, color = "black") +
                geom_sf(data = points_sf, aes(color = factor(Presence)), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
                scale_color_manual(
                    values = c("0" = "purple", "1" = "forestgreen"),
                    labels = c("0" = "Pseudo-absence", "1" = "Presence"),
                    name = "Point Type"
                ) +
                labs(
                    title = paste0(display_name, " Presence/Pseudo-absence Points"),
                    subtitle = paste0(
                        nrow(points$presence), " presence points, ", 
                        nrow(points$pseudo_absence), " pseudo-absence points (25km buffer)"
                    ),
                    x = "Longitude",
                    y = "Latitude"
                ) +
                theme_minimal() +
                theme(
                    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
                    legend.position = "bottom"
                ) +
                coord_sf()
      })
  
  # Evaluate spatial independence between replicate sets
      compare_replicates <- function(species_name) {
          replicates <- pseudo_absence_sets[[species_name]]
          
          # Calculate distances between points in different replicates
            distances <- map(1:(n_replicates-1), function(i) {
              rep_i <- replicates[[i]]$pseudo_absence
              rep_i_sf <- create_sf_points(rep_i)
              
              map((i+1):n_replicates, function(j) {
                  rep_j <- replicates[[j]]$pseudo_absence
                  rep_j_sf <- create_sf_points(rep_j)
                  
                  # Calculate minimum distances between points
                    min_dists <- st_nearest_feature(rep_i_sf, rep_j_sf)
                    dist_values <- st_distance(rep_i_sf, rep_j_sf[min_dists,], by_element = TRUE)
                  
                  # Return summary statistics
                    data.frame(
                        replicate_i = i,
                        replicate_j = j,
                        min_dist_m = as.numeric(min(dist_values)),
                        mean_dist_m = as.numeric(mean(dist_values)),
                        max_dist_m = as.numeric(max(dist_values))
                    )
              }) %>% bind_rows()
          }) %>% bind_rows()
          
          return(distances)
      }
```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 9. COVARIATE EXTRACTION FOR PRESENCE/PSEUDO-ABSENCE DATASETS
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r COVARIATE EXTRACTION FOR PRESENCE/PSEUDO-ABSENCE}

  # Define function to extract environmental covariates for point data
      extract_covariates <- function(points_df, cov_stack) {
          # Convert to spatial features for extraction
            points_sf <- st_as_sf(
                points_df, 
                coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), 
                crs = st_crs(cov_stack)
            )
          
          # Extract covariates from raster stack
            cov_values <- raster::extract(cov_stack, points_sf)
          
          # Combine with original data
            result <- cbind(points_df, as.data.frame(cov_values))
          
          # Convert landcover_class to factor if present
            if ("landcover_class" %in% names(result)) {
                result$landcover_class <- as.factor(result$landcover_class)
            }
          
          return(result)
      }
  
  # Ensure environmental data is in appropriate format
      if (inherits(CovStack, "SpatRaster")) {
          cov_stack <- stack(CovStack)
      } else {
          cov_stack <- CovStack
      }
  
  # Generate processing queue for all species/replicate combinations
      species_replicate_combos <- cross_df(list(
          species_idx = 1:length(species_with_sufficient_presence),
          rep_num = 1:n_replicates
      ))
  
  # Process all combinations to create finalized modeling datasets
      all_results <- map(1:nrow(species_replicate_combos), function(i) {
          # Get species and replicate metadata
            combo <- species_replicate_combos[i, ]
            species_idx <- combo$species_idx
            rep_num <- combo$rep_num
            species_name <- species_with_sufficient_presence[species_idx]
            abbr <- name_mapping[species_name]
          
          # Retrieve presence data with environmental covariates
            dataset_name <- paste0(abbr, "_BRTData")
            original_dataset <- get(dataset_name)
            presence_data <- original_dataset %>% filter(Presence == 1)
          
          # Get pseudo-absence coordinates for this replicate
            replicate_name <- paste0("replicate_", rep_num)
            pseudo_absence_points <- pseudo_absence_sets[[species_name]][[replicate_name]]$pseudo_absence
          
          # Extract environmental covariates for pseudo-absence points
            pseudo_absence_data <- extract_covariates(pseudo_absence_points, cov_stack)
          
          # Merge presence and pseudo-absence data
            complete_dataset <- bind_rows(presence_data, pseudo_absence_data)
          
          # Create standardized dataset name
            replicate_dataset_name <- paste0(abbr, "_PseudoAbs_", rep_num)
          
          # Store dataset in global environment
            assign(replicate_dataset_name, complete_dataset, envir = .GlobalEnv)
          
          # Release memory
            gc(verbose = FALSE)
          
          # Return dataset metadata
            list(
                dataset = complete_dataset,
                dataset_name = replicate_dataset_name,
                species_name = species_name,
                replicate = rep_num,
                n_presence = nrow(presence_data),
                n_pseudo = nrow(pseudo_absence_data)
            )
      })
  
  # Generate summary statistics for created datasets
      pseudo_datasets_summary <- map_dfr(all_results, function(result) {
          tibble(
              Species = gsub("_", " ", result$species_name),
              Replicate = result$replicate,
              Dataset_Name = result$dataset_name,
              Rows = nrow(result$dataset),
              Columns = ncol(result$dataset),
              Presence_Points = result$n_presence,
              Pseudo_Absence_Points = result$n_pseudo,
              Buffer_Distance_km = 25
          )
      })
  
  # Data cleaning: Remove extraneous variables
      pseudo_abs_datasets <- ls(pattern = "_PseudoAbs_\\d+$", envir = .GlobalEnv)
      
      invisible(
          map(pseudo_abs_datasets, function(dataset_name) {
              # Get dataset
                dataset <- get(dataset_name)
              
              # Remove X variable if present (artifact of spatial extraction)
                if ("X" %in% names(dataset)) {
                    dataset <- dataset %>% dplyr::select(-X)
                    assign(dataset_name, dataset, envir = .GlobalEnv)
                }
              
              return(NULL)
          })
      )

```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 10. LOAD IN CREATED REAL-PRESENCE BRT DATASETS
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r LOAD IN CREATED REAL-PRESENCE BRT DATASETS}

  # Base URL for raw GitHub content
      base_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IanPsheaSmith/GeorgiaTickDist/main/BRT_RealAbs_Datasets/"
  
  # Create directory for downloaded files
    if (!dir.exists("BRT_Data")) dir.create("BRT_Data")
  
  # Download and import individual datasets
  
    # Ixodes ricinus
      Ixric_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Ixric_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Ixric_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Ixric_BRTData$landcover_class)
    
    # Dermacentor reticulatus
      Deret_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Deret_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Deret_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Deret_BRTData$landcover_class)
    
    # Dermacentor marginatus
      Demar_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Demar_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Demar_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Demar_BRTData$landcover_class)
      
    # Haemaphysalis parva
      Hapar_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Hapar_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Hapar_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Hapar_BRTData$landcover_class)
      
    # Haemaphysalis punctata
      Hapun_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Hapun_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Hapun_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Hapun_BRTData$landcover_class)
      
    # Haemaphysalis inermis
      Haine_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Haine_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Haine_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Haine_BRTData$landcover_class)
      
    # Rhipicephalus bursa
      Rhbur_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Rhbur_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Rhbur_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Rhbur_BRTData$landcover_class)
      
    # Rhipicephalus annulatus
      Rhann_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Rhann_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Rhann_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Rhann_BRTData$landcover_class)
      
    # Rhipicephalus rossicus
      Rhros_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Rhros_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Rhros_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Rhros_BRTData$landcover_class)
      
    # Rhipicephalus turanicus
      Rhtur_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Rhtur_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Rhtur_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Rhtur_BRTData$landcover_class)
    
    # Hyalomma marginatum
      Hymar_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Hymar_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Hymar_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Hymar_BRTData$landcover_class)
      
    # Haemaphysalis sulcata
      Hasul_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Hasul_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Hasul_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Hasul_BRTData$landcover_class)
    
    # Hyalomma aegyptium
      Hyaeg_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Hyaeg_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Hyaeg_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Hyaeg_BRTData$landcover_class)
      
    # Rhipicephalus sanguineus
      Rhsan_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Rhsan_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Rhsan_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Rhsan_BRTData$landcover_class)
    
    # Haemaphysalis concinna
      Hacon_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Hacon_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Hacon_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Hacon_BRTData$landcover_class)
    
    # Haemaphysalis caucasica
      Hacau_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Hacau_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Hacau_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Hacau_BRTData$landcover_class)
      
    # Haemaphysalis erinacei
      Haeri_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Haeri_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Haeri_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Haeri_BRTData$landcover_class)
      
    # Hyalomma impeltatum
      Hyimp_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Hyimp_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Hyimp_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Hyimp_BRTData$landcover_class)
    
    # Ixodes acuminatus
      Ixacu_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Ixacu_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Ixacu_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Ixacu_BRTData$landcover_class)
    
    # Rhipicephalus pusillus
      Rhpus_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Rhpus_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Rhpus_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Rhpus_BRTData$landcover_class)
    
    # Hyalomma scupense
      Hyscu_BRTData <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "Hyscu_BRTData.csv"))[, -1]
      Hyscu_BRTData$landcover_class <- as.factor(Hyscu_BRTData$landcover_class)
    
  # Download summary file
    BRTData_Summary <- read.csv(paste0(base_url, "BRTData_Summary.csv"))[, -1]


```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 11. LOAD IN CREATED PSEUDO-ABSENCE BRT DATASETS
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r LOAD IN CREATED PSEUDO-ABSENCE BRT DATASETS}


  # Base URL for raw GitHub content
  pseudo_abs_base_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IanPsheaSmith/GeorgiaTickDist/main/BRT_PseudoAbs_Datasets/"
  
  # Create directory for downloaded files
  if (!dir.exists("PseudoAbs_BRT_Data")) dir.create("PseudoAbs_BRT_Data")
  
  # Load summary file first to get dataset names
  PseudoAbs_Summary <- read.csv(paste0(pseudo_abs_base_url, "pseudoabsence_datasets_summary.csv"))
  
  # Function to load a pseudo-absence dataset
  load_pseudo_abs_dataset <- function(dataset_name) {
    # Construct file path
    file_path <- paste0(pseudo_abs_base_url, dataset_name, ".csv")
    
    # Read data
    dataset <- read.csv(file_path)
    
    # Convert landcover_class to factor
    if ("landcover_class" %in% names(dataset)) {
      dataset$landcover_class <- as.factor(dataset$landcover_class)
    }
    
    # Return dataset
    return(dataset)
  }

  # Define species with pseudo-absence datasets
  pseudo_abs_species <- c(
    "Demar", "Deret", "Haine", "Hapar", "Hapun", "Hasul", 
    "Hymar", "Ixric", "Rhann", "Rhbur", "Rhros", "Rhtur"
  )
  
  # Generate all dataset names
  all_dataset_names <- map(pseudo_abs_species, function(sp) {
    map_chr(1:10, function(i) paste0(sp, "_PseudoAbs_", i))
  }) %>% unlist()
  
  # Load all datasets and assign to global environment
  invisible(
    map(all_dataset_names, function(dataset_name) {
      dataset <- load_pseudo_abs_dataset(dataset_name)
      assign(dataset_name, dataset, envir = .GlobalEnv)
      dataset_name
    })
  )
  
  # Create list for each species containing all 10 replicates
  species_lists <- map(pseudo_abs_species, function(sp) {
    # Create list name
    list_name <- paste0(sp, "_PseudoAbs_List")
    
    # Create list of datasets
    dataset_list <- map(1:10, function(i) {
      dataset_name <- paste0(sp, "_PseudoAbs_", i)
      get(dataset_name)
    })
    
    # Assign list to global environment
    assign(list_name, dataset_list, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    
    # Return list name
    list_name
  })

  # Print summary of loaded datasets
  cat("Loaded", nrow(PseudoAbs_Summary), "pseudo-absence datasets\n")
  cat("Created dataset lists for", length(pseudo_abs_species), "species\n")
  
  # Count total number of presence points across all replicates
  species_summary <- PseudoAbs_Summary %>%
    group_by(Species) %>%
    summarize(
      Replicates = n_distinct(Replicate),
      Presence_Points = first(Presence_Points),
      Pseudo_Absence_Points = first(Pseudo_Absence_Points),
      Total_Points = first(Presence_Points) + first(Pseudo_Absence_Points)
    )
  
  print(species_summary)

  # Create a convenient function to access datasets by species name and replicate
  get_pseudoabs_dataset <- function(species_abbr, replicate_num = 1) {
    if (!species_abbr %in% pseudo_abs_species) {
      stop("Species abbreviation not found. Available species: ", 
           paste(pseudo_abs_species, collapse=", "))
    }
    if (!replicate_num %in% 1:10) {
      stop("Replicate number must be between 1 and 10")
    }
    
    dataset_name <- paste0(species_abbr, "_PseudoAbs_", replicate_num)
    return(get(dataset_name))
  }

```
  


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 12. BRTs USING TRUE PRESENCE DATA
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r BRTs USING TRUE PRESENCE DATA}

  # Configure parallel processing environment
      cl <- makeCluster(10)
      registerDoParallel(cl)
  
  # Define comprehensive function for species distribution modeling
      run_species_brt <- function(species_code) {
          # Load required libraries within each worker
          library(dplyr)
          library(gbm)
          library(raster)
          
          # Interpret species code for display
          genus_part <- substr(species_code, 1, 2)
          species_part <- substr(species_code, 3, 5)
          
          # Determine full genus name
          species_display <- paste(
              case_when(
                  genus_part == "De" ~ "Dermacentor",
                  genus_part == "Hy" ~ "Hyalomma", 
                  genus_part == "Ix" ~ "Ixodes",
                  genus_part == "Ha" ~ "Haemaphysalis",
                  genus_part == "Rh" ~ "Rhipicephalus",
                  TRUE ~ genus_part
              ),
              species_part
          )
          
          # Retrieve and prepare modeling dataset
          dataset_name <- paste0(species_code, "_BRTData")
          
          if (!exists(dataset_name)) {
              stop(paste("Dataset", dataset_name, "not found in global environment"))
          }
          
          raw_data <- get(dataset_name)
          
          # Format categorical variables and ensure proper data types
          formatted_data <- raw_data %>%
              mutate(
                  landcover_class = as.factor(landcover_class),
                  Presence = as.numeric(Presence)
              )
          
          # Remove spatial coordinates from analysis
          cols_to_keep <- !names(formatted_data) %in% c("Longitude", "Latitude")
          formatted_data <- formatted_data[, cols_to_keep]
          
          # Handle missing values
          clean_data <- na.omit(formatted_data)
          
          # Verify sufficient presence records for modeling
          n_presences <- sum(clean_data$Presence)
          if (n_presences <= 15) {
              return(list(
                  species_code = species_code,
                  species_display = species_display,
                  skipped = TRUE,
                  reason = paste("Insufficient presences:", n_presences, "(<= 15)")
              ))
          }
          
          # Partition data for model training and evaluation
          set.seed(1999)
          train_indices <- sample(seq_len(nrow(clean_data)), 
                                 size = floor(0.75 * nrow(clean_data)))
          
          train_data <- clean_data[train_indices, ]
          test_data <- clean_data[-train_indices, ]
          
          # Apply differential weighting to balance classes
          train_weights <- ifelse(train_data$Presence == 1, 1, 0.5)
          
          # Define model fitting function
          fit_brt_model <- function(data, weights, predictors = NULL) {
              if (is.null(predictors)) {
                  # Use all variables except Presence
                  formula_str <- "Presence ~ ."
                  model_data <- data
              } else {
                  # Use only specified predictors
                  model_data <- data[, c("Presence", predictors)]
                  formula_str <- "Presence ~ ."
              }
              
              brt_model <- gbm(
                  as.formula(formula_str),
                  data = model_data,
                  distribution = "bernoulli",
                  n.trees = 2500,
                  interaction.depth = 5,
                  shrinkage = 0.005,
                  bag.fraction = 0.5,
                  cv.folds = 10,
                  weights = weights,
                  n.minobsinnode = 10,
                  verbose = FALSE
              )
              
              return(brt_model)
          }
          
          # Fit initial model
          initial_model <- fit_brt_model(train_data, train_weights)
          best_iter <- gbm.perf(initial_model, method = "cv", plot.it = FALSE)
          
          # Variable importance optimization
          current_model <- initial_model
          current_predictors <- names(train_data)[names(train_data) != "Presence"]
          iteration <- 0
          initial_cv_error <- min(current_model$cv.error)
          
          # Initialize tracking variables
          removed_variables <- character(0)
          importance_history <- data.frame(
              iteration = 0, 
              variables_count = length(current_predictors),
              min_importance = NA, 
              cv_error = initial_cv_error,
              variables_removed = ""
          )
          
          # Iterative variable selection process
          repeat {
              iteration <- iteration + 1
              
              # Assess variable importance
              rel_inf <- summary(current_model, plot = FALSE)
              min_importance <- min(rel_inf$rel.inf)
              
              # Identify variables with < 1% importance
              low_importance_vars <- rel_inf[rel_inf$rel.inf < 1.0, "var"]
              
              # Termination criteria
              if (length(low_importance_vars) == 0 ||
                  length(current_predictors) <= 3 ||
                  iteration > 30) {
                  break
              }
              
              # Remove least influential variable
              var_to_remove <- low_importance_vars[which.min(
                  rel_inf[rel_inf$var %in% low_importance_vars, "rel.inf"]
              )]
              
              # Update predictors and rebuild model
              current_predictors <- setdiff(current_predictors, var_to_remove)
              temp_model <- fit_brt_model(train_data, train_weights, current_predictors)
              
              # Update tracking information
              current_model <- temp_model
              removed_variables <- c(removed_variables, var_to_remove)
              
              importance_history <- rbind(importance_history, 
                                        data.frame(
                                            iteration = iteration,
                                            variables_count = length(current_predictors),
                                            min_importance = min_importance,
                                            cv_error = min(temp_model$cv.error),
                                            variables_removed = var_to_remove
                                        ))
          }
          
          # Finalize optimized model
          final_model <- current_model
          final_optimal_trees <- gbm.perf(final_model, method = "cv", plot.it = FALSE)
          final_importance <- summary(final_model, plot = FALSE)
          
          # Calculate model performance metrics
          train_preds <- predict(final_model, train_data, n.trees = final_optimal_trees, 
                                type = "response")
          test_preds <- predict(final_model, test_data, n.trees = final_optimal_trees, 
                               type = "response")
          
          train_rmse <- sqrt(mean((train_data$Presence - train_preds)^2))
          test_rmse <- sqrt(mean((test_data$Presence - test_preds)^2))
          
          # Generate spatial predictions
          predictions_raster <- predict(
              CovStack,
              final_model,
              n.trees = final_optimal_trees,
              type = "response"
          )
          
          # Export key results to global environment
          assign(paste0(species_code, "_final_model"), final_model, envir = .GlobalEnv)
          assign(paste0(species_code, "_predictions"), predictions_raster, envir = .GlobalEnv)
          assign(paste0(species_code, "_importance"), final_importance, envir = .GlobalEnv)
          
          # Return comprehensive results
          list(
              species_code = species_code,
              species_display = species_display,
              final_model = final_model,
              optimal_trees = final_optimal_trees,
              final_importance = final_importance,
              removed_variables = removed_variables,
              importance_history = importance_history,
              performance = list(
                  train_rmse = train_rmse,
                  test_rmse = test_rmse,
                  cv_error = min(final_model$cv.error)
              ),
              predictions_raster = predictions_raster,
              training_data = train_data,
              testing_data = test_data
          )
      }
  
  # Identify species for model development
      original_species <- c(
          "Ixodes_ricinus", "Dermacentor_reticulatus", "Dermacentor_marginatus",
          "Haemaphysalis_parva", "Haemaphysalis_punctata", "Haemaphysalis_inermis", 
          "Rhipicephalus_bursa", "Rhipicephalus_annulatus", "Rhipicephalus_rossicus", 
          "Rhipicephalus_turanicus", "Hyalomma_marginatum", "Haemaphysalis_sulcata", 
          "Hyalomma_aegyptium", "Rhipicephalus_sanguineus", "Haemaphysalis_concinna", 
          "Haemaphysalis_caucasica", "Haemaphysalis_erinacei", "Hyalomma_impeltatum",
          "Ixodes_acuminatus", "Rhipicephalus_pusillus", "Hyalomma_scupense"
      )
      
      # Convert to standardized species codes
      species_codes <- map_chr(original_species, function(sp) {
          parts <- strsplit(sp, "_")[[1]]
          genus <- substr(parts[1], 1, 2)
          species <- substr(parts[2], 1, 3)
          paste0(genus, species)
      })
  
  # Execute modeling for all species using parallel processing
      all_results <- species_codes %>%
          map(~ {
              tryCatch({
                  run_species_brt(.x)
              }, error = function(e) {
                  return(NULL)
              })
          })
      
      # Name results for reference
      names(all_results) <- species_codes
      
      # Categorize modeling outcomes
      successful_results <- all_results[!map_lgl(all_results, is.null)]
      skipped_results <- successful_results[map_lgl(successful_results, 
                                            ~ !is.null(.x$skipped) && .x$skipped)]
      successful_results <- successful_results[!map_lgl(successful_results, 
                                              ~ !is.null(.x$skipped) && .x$skipped)]
  
  # Compile performance summary
      summary_table <- successful_results %>%
          map_dfr(~ {
              data.frame(
                  species_code = .x$species_code,
                  species_display = .x$species_display,
                  final_variables = nrow(.x$final_importance),
                  variables_removed = length(.x$removed_variables),
                  train_rmse = .x$performance$train_rmse,
                  test_rmse = .x$performance$test_rmse,
                  cv_error = .x$performance$cv_error,
                  optimal_trees = .x$optimal_trees
              )
          })
  
  # Create visualizations of model performance
      if (nrow(summary_table) > 0) {
          # Performance comparison across species
          performance_plot <- ggplot(summary_table, aes(x = species_code)) +
              geom_col(aes(y = test_rmse), fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.7) +
              geom_point(aes(y = train_rmse), color = "red", size = 2) +
              labs(
                  title = "Model Performance Summary",
                  subtitle = "Bars = Test RMSE, Points = Train RMSE",
                  x = "Species Code",
                  y = "RMSE"
              ) +
              theme_minimal() +
              theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
          
          print(performance_plot)
          
          # Model complexity visualization
          var_count_plot <- ggplot(summary_table, aes(x = species_code)) +
              geom_col(aes(y = final_variables), fill = "orchid", alpha = 0.7) +
              labs(
                  title = "Final Variable Count by Species",
                  x = "Species Code",
                  y = "Number of Variables in Final Model"
              ) +
              theme_minimal() +
              theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
          
          print(var_count_plot)
      }
  
  # Release parallel processing resources
      stopCluster(cl)
```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 13. ENSEMBLE BRTs W/ PSEUDOABSENCES
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r ENSEMBLE BRTs W/ PSEUDOABSENCES}

  # Configure parallel processing environment for computational efficiency
      cl <- makeCluster(15)
      registerDoParallel(cl)
  
  # Define comprehensive function for ensemble species distribution modeling
      run_species_ensemble_brt <- function(species_code, n_replicates = 10) {
          # Load required libraries within each worker
            library(dplyr)
            library(gbm)
            library(raster)
          
          # Interpret species code for display
            genus_part <- substr(species_code, 1, 2)
            species_part <- substr(species_code, 3, 5)
          
          # Determine full genus name
            species_display <- paste(
                case_when(
                    genus_part == "De" ~ "Dermacentor",
                    genus_part == "Hy" ~ "Hyalomma", 
                    genus_part == "Ix" ~ "Ixodes",
                    genus_part == "Ha" ~ "Haemaphysalis",
                    genus_part == "Rh" ~ "Rhipicephalus",
                    TRUE ~ genus_part
                ),
                species_part
            )
          
          # Verify dataset availability
            replicate_datasets <- paste0(species_code, "_PseudoAbs_", 1:n_replicates)
            existing_datasets <- replicate_datasets[sapply(replicate_datasets, exists)]
          
          if (length(existing_datasets) == 0) {
              return(list(
                  species_code = species_code,
                  species_display = species_display,
                  skipped = TRUE,
                  reason = "No pseudo-absence datasets found"
              ))
          }
          
          # Define model fitting function
            fit_brt_model <- function(data, weights, predictors = NULL) {
                if (is.null(predictors)) {
                    # Use all variables except Presence
                      formula_str <- "Presence ~ ."
                      model_data <- data
                } else {
                    # Use only specified predictors
                      model_data <- data[, c("Presence", predictors)]
                      formula_str <- "Presence ~ ."
                }
                
                brt_model <- gbm(
                    as.formula(formula_str),
                    data = model_data,
                    distribution = "bernoulli",
                    n.trees = 2500,
                    interaction.depth = 5,
                    shrinkage = 0.005,
                    bag.fraction = 0.5,
                    cv.folds = 10,
                    weights = weights,
                    n.minobsinnode = 10,
                    verbose = FALSE
                )
                
                return(brt_model)
          }
          
          # Process each replicate dataset
            replicate_results <- list()
            all_importance_vars <- character(0)
            
            for (i in seq_along(existing_datasets)) {
                dataset_name <- existing_datasets[i]
                replicate_num <- i
              
              # Load and preprocess dataset
                raw_data <- get(dataset_name)
                formatted_data <- raw_data %>%
                    mutate(
                        landcover_class = as.factor(landcover_class),
                        Presence = as.numeric(Presence)
                    )
              
              # Remove spatial coordinates
                cols_to_keep <- !names(formatted_data) %in% c("Longitude", "Latitude")
                formatted_data <- formatted_data[, cols_to_keep]
              
              # Handle missing values
                clean_data <- na.omit(formatted_data)
              
              # Verify sufficient presence records
                n_presences <- sum(clean_data$Presence)
                if (n_presences <= 15) {
                    next
                }
              
              # Partition data for model training and evaluation
                set.seed(1999 + replicate_num)
                train_indices <- sample(seq_len(nrow(clean_data)), 
                                      size = floor(0.75 * nrow(clean_data)))
                
                train_data <- clean_data[train_indices, ]
                test_data <- clean_data[-train_indices, ]
              
              # Apply differential weighting to balance classes
                train_weights <- ifelse(train_data$Presence == 1, 1, 0.5)
              
              # Fit initial model
                initial_model <- fit_brt_model(train_data, train_weights)
                best_iter <- gbm.perf(initial_model, method = "cv", plot.it = FALSE)
              
              # Variable importance optimization
                current_model <- initial_model
                current_predictors <- names(train_data)[names(train_data) != "Presence"]
                iteration <- 0
                initial_cv_error <- min(current_model$cv.error)
                removed_variables <- character(0)
                
              # Iterative variable selection process
                repeat {
                    iteration <- iteration + 1
                  
                  # Assess variable importance
                    rel_inf <- summary(current_model, plot = FALSE)
                    low_importance_vars <- rel_inf[rel_inf$rel.inf < 1.0, "var"]
                  
                  # Termination criteria
                    if (length(low_importance_vars) == 0 || 
                        length(current_predictors) <= 3 || 
                        iteration > 30) {
                        break
                    }
                  
                  # Remove least influential variable
                    var_to_remove <- low_importance_vars[which.min(
                        rel_inf[rel_inf$var %in% low_importance_vars, "rel.inf"]
                    )]
                    current_predictors <- setdiff(current_predictors, var_to_remove)
                    current_model <- fit_brt_model(train_data, train_weights, current_predictors)
                    removed_variables <- c(removed_variables, var_to_remove)
              }
              
              # Evaluate final model
                final_model <- current_model
                final_optimal_trees <- gbm.perf(final_model, method = "cv", plot.it = FALSE)
```
