```{r ENSEMBLE BRTs W/ PSEUDOABSENCES}
                final_importance <- summary(final_model, plot = FALSE)
              
              # Calculate performance metrics
                train_preds <- predict(final_model, train_data, n.trees = final_optimal_trees, 
                                      type = "response")
                test_preds <- predict(final_model, test_data, n.trees = final_optimal_trees, 
                                     type = "response")
                
                train_rmse <- sqrt(mean((train_data$Presence - train_preds)^2))
                test_rmse <- sqrt(mean((test_data$Presence - test_preds)^2))
              
              # Generate spatial predictions
                predictions_raster <- predict(
                    CovStack,
                    final_model,
                    n.trees = final_optimal_trees,
                    type = "response"
                )
              
              # Store replicate results
                replicate_results[[replicate_num]] <- list(
                    replicate = replicate_num,
                    dataset_name = dataset_name,
                    final_model = final_model,
                    optimal_trees = final_optimal_trees,
                    final_importance = final_importance,
                    removed_variables = removed_variables,
                    performance = list(
                        train_rmse = train_rmse,
                        test_rmse = test_rmse,
                        cv_error = min(final_model$cv.error)
                    ),
                    predictions_raster = predictions_raster,
                    n_variables = nrow(final_importance),
                    n_presences = n_presences
                )
              
              # Track important variables for ensemble analysis
                all_importance_vars <- unique(c(all_importance_vars, final_importance$var))
          }
          
          # Filter successful replicates
            successful_replicates <- replicate_results[!sapply(replicate_results, is.null)]
            
            if (length(successful_replicates) == 0) {
                return(list(
                    species_code = species_code,
                    species_display = species_display,
                    skipped = TRUE,
                    reason = "No successful replicates"
                ))
          }
          
          # Generate ensemble predictions
            prediction_rasters <- map(successful_replicates, ~ .x$predictions_raster)
            ensemble_raster <- stack(prediction_rasters)
            ensemble_mean <- calc(ensemble_raster, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
            ensemble_sd <- calc(ensemble_raster, fun = sd, na.rm = TRUE)
          
          # Calculate ensemble variable importance
            all_importance <- map_dfr(successful_replicates, ~ {
                .x$final_importance %>%
                    mutate(replicate = .x$replicate)
            })
            
            ensemble_importance <- all_importance %>%
                group_by(var) %>%
                summarise(
                    rel.inf = mean(rel.inf, na.rm = TRUE),
                    frequency = n(),
                    .groups = "drop"
                ) %>%
                arrange(desc(rel.inf))
          
          # Compile performance metrics
            performance_summary <- map_dfr(successful_replicates, ~ {
                data.frame(
                    replicate = .x$replicate,
                    train_rmse = .x$performance$train_rmse,
                    test_rmse = .x$performance$test_rmse,
                    cv_error = .x$performance$cv_error,
                    n_variables = .x$n_variables,
                    n_presences = .x$n_presences
                )
            })
            
            ensemble_performance <- list(
                mean_train_rmse = mean(performance_summary$train_rmse),
                mean_test_rmse = mean(performance_summary$test_rmse),
                mean_cv_error = mean(performance_summary$cv_error),
                sd_train_rmse = sd(performance_summary$train_rmse),
                sd_test_rmse = sd(performance_summary$test_rmse),
                sd_cv_error = sd(performance_summary$cv_error)
            )
          
          # Export key results to global environment
            assign(paste0(species_code, "_ensemble_predictions"), 
                   ensemble_mean, envir = .GlobalEnv)
            assign(paste0(species_code, "_ensemble_uncertainty"), 
                   ensemble_sd, envir = .GlobalEnv)
            assign(paste0(species_code, "_ensemble_importance"), 
                   ensemble_importance, envir = .GlobalEnv)
            assign(paste0(species_code, "_replicate_results"), 
                   successful_replicates, envir = .GlobalEnv)
          
          # Return comprehensive results
            list(
                species_code = species_code,
                species_display = species_display,
                n_successful_replicates = length(successful_replicates),
                replicate_results = successful_replicates,
                ensemble_predictions = ensemble_mean,
                ensemble_uncertainty = ensemble_sd,
                ensemble_importance = ensemble_importance,
                performance_summary = performance_summary,
                ensemble_performance = ensemble_performance,
                all_importance_data = all_importance
            )
      }
  
  # Identify species for model development
      pseudo_abs_datasets <- ls(pattern = "_PseudoAbs_\\d+$", envir = .GlobalEnv)
      species_codes <- unique(gsub("_PseudoAbs_\\d+$", "", pseudo_abs_datasets))
  
  # Execute ensemble modeling for all species
      ensemble_results <- species_codes %>%
          map(~ {
              tryCatch({
                  run_species_ensemble_brt(.x, n_replicates = 10)
              }, error = function(e) {
                  return(NULL)
              })
          })
      
      # Name results for reference
        names(ensemble_results) <- species_codes
      
      # Categorize modeling outcomes
        successful_ensemble_results <- ensemble_results[!map_lgl(ensemble_results, is.null)]
        skipped_ensemble_results <- successful_ensemble_results[
            map_lgl(successful_ensemble_results, ~ !is.null(.x$skipped) && .x$skipped)]
        successful_ensemble_results <- successful_ensemble_results[
            !map_lgl(successful_ensemble_results, ~ !is.null(.x$skipped) && .x$skipped)]
  
  # Compile comprehensive performance summary
      ensemble_summary_table <- successful_ensemble_results %>%
          map_dfr(~ {
              data.frame(
                  species_code = .x$species_code,
                  species_display = .x$species_display,
                  successful_replicates = .x$n_successful_replicates,
                  mean_test_rmse = .x$ensemble_performance$mean_test_rmse,
                  sd_test_rmse = .x$ensemble_performance$sd_test_rmse,
                  mean_cv_error = .x$ensemble_performance$mean_cv_error,
                  sd_cv_error = .x$ensemble_performance$sd_cv_error,
                  top_variable = .x$ensemble_importance$var[1],
                  top_variable_importance = .x$ensemble_importance$rel.inf[1]
              )
          })
  
  # Create visualizations of model performance
      if (nrow(ensemble_summary_table) > 0) {
          # Performance comparison across species
            performance_plot <- ggplot(ensemble_summary_table, aes(x = species_code)) +
                geom_col(aes(y = mean_test_rmse), fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.7) +
                geom_errorbar(
                    aes(ymin = mean_test_rmse - sd_test_rmse, 
                        ymax = mean_test_rmse + sd_test_rmse), 
                    width = 0.2, color = "red"
                ) +
                labs(
                    title = "Ensemble Model Performance",
                    subtitle = "Mean Test RMSE ± SD across replicates",
                    x = "Species Code",
                    y = "Test RMSE"
                ) +
                theme_minimal() +
                theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
            
            print(performance_plot)
          
          # Replicate success visualization
            replicate_plot <- ggplot(ensemble_summary_table, aes(x = species_code)) +
                geom_col(aes(y = successful_replicates), fill = "orchid", alpha = 0.7) +
                geom_hline(yintercept = 10, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
                labs(
                    title = "Successful Replicates per Species",
                    x = "Species Code",
                    y = "Number of Successful Replicates"
                ) +
                theme_minimal() +
                theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
            
            print(replicate_plot)
      }
  
  # Analyze variable importance patterns across species
      if (length(successful_ensemble_results) > 0) {
          all_variables_summary <- successful_ensemble_results %>%
              map_dfr(~ {
                  .x$ensemble_importance %>%
                      mutate( species_code = .x$species_code,
                          species_display = .x$species_display
                      )
              }) %>%
              group_by(var) %>%
              summarise(
                  mean_importance = mean(rel.inf, na.rm = TRUE),
                  sd_importance = sd(rel.inf, na.rm = TRUE),
                  species_count = n(),
                  max_importance = max(rel.inf, na.rm = TRUE),
                  .groups = "drop"
              ) %>%
              arrange(desc(mean_importance))
      }
  
  # Release parallel processing resources
      stopCluster(cl)
      
```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 14. CLUSTGEO IMPLEMENTATION FOR TICK SPECIES BIOREGIONALIZATION
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r CLUSTGEO GEORGIA/TICKS BIOREGIONALIZATION}

  # ==============================================================================
  # STEP 1: DATA PREPARATION AND QUALITY CONTROL
  # ==============================================================================

    # Load and combine all species presence data
        species_list <- c(
            "Ixodes ricinus", "Dermacentor reticulatus", "Dermacentor marginatus",
            "Haemaphysalis parva", "Haemaphysalis punctata", "Haemaphysalis inermis",
            "Rhipicephalus bursa", "Rhipicephalus annulatus", "Rhipicephalus rossicus", 
            "Rhipicephalus turanicus", "Hyalomma marginatum", "Haemaphysalis sulcata",
            "Hyalomma aegyptium", "Rhipicephalus sanguineus", "Haemaphysalis concinna",
            "Haemaphysalis caucasica", "Haemaphysalis erinacei", "Hyalomma impeltatum",
            "Ixodes acuminatus", "Rhipicephalus pusillus", "Hyalomma scupense"
        )
  
    # Combine all species presence data
        species_data_list <- purrr::map(species_list, function(species) {
            object_name <- paste0(gsub(" ", "_", species), "_presence")
            if (exists(object_name, inherits = TRUE)) {
                obj <- get(object_name, inherits = TRUE)
                obj$species <- species
                return(obj)
            } else {
                return(NULL)
            }
        }) %>% 
            purrr::compact()
  
        if (length(species_data_list) == 0) {
            stop("No species presence data found. Check object names.")
        }
  
        combined_species_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(species_data_list)
  
        cat("Loaded data for", length(species_data_list), "species\n")
        cat("Total presence records:", nrow(combined_species_data), "\n")

  # ==============================================================================
  # STEP 2: CREATE SITE-SPECIES MATRIX
  # ==============================================================================

    set.seed(1999)  # Reproducibility for kmeans and downstream steps

    # Extract coordinates for clustering
        coords <- sf::st_coordinates(combined_species_data)
        coords_df <- data.frame(
            x = coords[,1],
            y = coords[,2],
            species = combined_species_data$species,
            record_id = 1:nrow(combined_species_data)
        )
  
    # Set a range of possible site counts (clusters)
        possible_sites <- seq(10, 100, by = 5)
          # Fewer than 10 is not ecologically or probabilistically reasonable
          # given the size of the study area and the number of presence locations
  
        site_diag <- purrr::map_dfr(possible_sites, function(k) {
            km <- kmeans(coords_df[,1:2], centers = k, nstart = 50)
            coords_df$site_id <- paste0("Site_", km$cluster)
            # Create site-species incidence
              mat <- combined_species_data %>%
                  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
                  dplyr::mutate(site_id = coords_df$site_id) %>%
                  dplyr::group_by(site_id, species) %>%
                  dplyr::summarise(presence = max(Presence), .groups = 'drop') %>%
                  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = species, values_from = presence, values_fill = 0)
              
              # Fix tibble rownames warning - convert to data.frame first
                mat <- as.data.frame(mat)
                rownames(mat) <- mat$site_id
                mat <- mat %>% dplyr::select(-site_id) %>% as.matrix()
                n_sites <- nrow(mat)
              
            # Species richness and site sizes
              site_richness <- rowSums(mat)
              min_rich <- min(site_richness)
              mean_rich <- mean(site_richness)
              min_size <- min(table(coords_df$site_id))
              mean_size <- mean(table(coords_df$site_id))
            # WSS (geographic)
              wss <- sum(km$withinss)
              tibble::tibble(
                  k = k,
                  n_sites = n_sites,
                  mean_richness = mean_rich,
                  min_richness = min_rich,
                  mean_size = mean_size,
                  min_size = min_size,
                  wss = wss
              )
        })
  
    # Plot diagnostics to choose best number of sites
        p1_site <- ggplot(site_diag, aes(x = k, y = wss)) +
            geom_line() + geom_point() + labs(y = "Geographic WSS", x = "Number of Sites (k)")
        p2_site <- ggplot(site_diag, aes(x = k, y = mean_richness)) +
            geom_line() + geom_point() + labs(y = "Mean Species Richness per Site", x = "Number of Sites (k)")
        p3_site <- ggplot(site_diag, aes(x = k, y = min_size)) +
            geom_line() + geom_point() +
            labs(y = "Min Samples per Site", x = "Number of Sites (k)")
  
        print(p1_site | p2_site | p3_site)
  
    # ---- PICK A FINAL NUMBER OF SITES ----
        final_k_sites <- 25  # Based on Plots p1-p3
  
    # ---- RUN FINAL SITE DEFINITION ----
        km <- kmeans(coords_df[,1:2], centers = final_k_sites, nstart = 50)
        coords_df$site_id <- paste0("Site_", km$cluster)
        combined_species_data$site_id <- coords_df$site_id
  
    # ---- CREATE FINAL SITE-SPECIES MATRIX ----
        site_species_matrix <- combined_species_data %>%
            sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
            dplyr::group_by(site_id, species) %>%
            dplyr::summarise(presence = max(Presence), .groups = 'drop') %>%
            tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = species, values_from = presence, values_fill = 0)
        
        # Fix tibble rownames warning - convert to data.frame first
            site_names <- site_species_matrix$site_id
            site_species_matrix <- as.data.frame(site_species_matrix)
            site_species_matrix <- site_species_matrix %>%
                dplyr::select(-site_id) %>%
                as.matrix()
            rownames(site_species_matrix) <- site_names
  
        cat("Site-species matrix dimensions:", dim(site_species_matrix), "\n")
        cat("Species richness range:", range(rowSums(site_species_matrix)), "\n")

  # ==============================================================================
  # STEP 3: ENVIRONMENTAL DATA EXTRACTION
  # ==============================================================================

    # Calculate site centroids
        site_centroids <- coords_df %>%
            dplyr::group_by(site_id) %>%
            dplyr::summarise(
                centroid_x = mean(x),
                centroid_y = mean(y),
                n_records = dplyr::n(),
                .groups = 'drop'
            )
 
    # Extract environmental data for sites
        site_coords_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(
            site_centroids, 
            coords = c("centroid_x", "centroid_y"), 
            crs = sf::st_crs(combined_species_data)
        )
        env_data <- raster::extract(
          CovStack, 
          site_coords_sf, 
          buffer = 1000,      # 1000 meters
          fun = mean,        # mean across all cells in buffer
          na.rm = TRUE
        )
        rownames(env_data) <- site_centroids$site_id
  
    # Filter sites with complete environmental data
        complete_sites <- complete.cases(env_data)
        cat("Sites with complete environmental data:", sum(complete_sites), "of", nrow(env_data), "\n")
  
    # Subset matrices to complete sites only
        site_species_final <- site_species_matrix[complete_sites, ]
        env_data_final <- env_data[complete_sites, ]
        site_centroids_final <- site_centroids[complete_sites, ]
  
        cat("Final dataset:", nrow(site_species_final), "sites ×", ncol(site_species_final), "species\n")

  # ==============================================================================
  # STEP 4: COMPUTE DISSIMILARITY MATRICES
  # ==============================================================================

    # D0: Ecological dissimilarity based on species composition (Jaccard)
        D0 <- vegan::vegdist(site_species_final, method = "jaccard", binary = TRUE)
        cat("D0 (ecological): Jaccard dissimilarity on species composition\n")
        cat("  Range:", round(range(D0), 3), "\n")
  
    # D1: Geographical dissimilarity based on site centroids
        coords_final <- site_centroids_final[, c("centroid_x", "centroid_y")]
        D1_matrix <- geodist::geodist(coords_final, measure = "haversine") / 1000  # Convert to km
        D1 <- as.dist(D1_matrix)
        cat("D1 (geographical): Euclidean distance between site centroids\n")
        cat("  Range:", round(range(D1), 1), "km\n")

  # ==============================================================================
  # STEP 5: DETERMINE OPTIMAL NUMBER OF CLUSTERS (K)
  # ==============================================================================

    # Build baseline ecological clustering tree
        tree_eco <- ClustGeo::hclustgeo(D0)
  
    # Parameters for ensemble diagnostics
        k_range <- 2:min(15, nrow(site_species_final) - 1)
        min_cluster_size <- 3 # No fewer than 3 bioregions 
        min_mean_richness <- 2 # No regions with less than 2 species (mean) per site
  
    # Calculate ensemble diagnostics
        ensemble_diag <- purrr::map_dfr(k_range, function(k) {
          clusters <- cutree(tree_eco, k = k)
          cluster_assign <- split(1:length(clusters), clusters)
          
          # Within-cluster sum of squares
            wss <- sum(sapply(cluster_assign, function(ix) {
              if (length(ix) < 2) return(0)
              dmat <- as.matrix(vegan::vegdist(site_species_final[ix, ], method = "jaccard", binary = TRUE))
              sum(dmat^2) / (2 * length(ix))
            }))
          
          # Mean between-cluster Jaccard dissimilarity
            between_dissim <- NA
            if (length(cluster_assign) > 1) {
              cluster_means <- lapply(cluster_assign, function(ix) colMeans(site_species_final[ix, , drop = FALSE]))
              combos <- combn(seq_along(cluster_means), 2, simplify = FALSE)
              dissims <- sapply(combos, function(pair) {
                vegan::vegdist(rbind(cluster_means[[pair[1]]], cluster_means[[pair[2]]]), method="jaccard")
              })
              between_dissim <- mean(dissims)
            }
          
          # Cluster sizes and species richness
            cluster_sizes <- sapply(cluster_assign, length)
            mean_size <- mean(cluster_sizes)
            min_size <- min(cluster_sizes)
            richness <- sapply(cluster_assign, function(ix) mean(rowSums(site_species_final[ix, , drop = FALSE])))
            mean_rich <- mean(richness)
            min_rich <- min(richness)
          
          # Silhouette width
            sil_score <- tryCatch({
              mean(cluster::silhouette(clusters, vegan::vegdist(site_species_final, method = "jaccard", binary = TRUE))[, 3])
            }, error = function(e) NA)
          
          # Calinski-Harabasz index
            ch_score <- tryCatch({
              fpc::cluster.stats(vegan::vegdist(site_species_final, method = "jaccard", binary = TRUE), clusters)$ch
            }, error = function(e) NA)
          
            tibble::tibble(
              k = k,
              wss = wss,
              between_jaccard = between_dissim,
              mean_cluster_size = mean_size,
              min_cluster_size = min_size,
              mean_richness = mean_rich,
              min_richness = min_rich,
              silhouette = sil_score,
              calinski_harabasz = ch_score
            )
          })
  
    # Plot diagnostics
        p1 <- ggplot(ensemble_diag, aes(x = k, y = wss)) +
          geom_line() + geom_point() + labs(y = "Within-cluster sum of squares (WSS)", x = "Number of Clusters (k)")
        p2 <- ggplot(ensemble_diag, aes(x = k, y = between_jaccard)) +
          geom_line() + geom_point() + labs(y = "Mean Between-Cluster Jaccard", x = "Number of Clusters (k)")
        p3 <- ggplot(ensemble_diag, aes(x = k, y = min_cluster_size)) +
          geom_line() + geom_point() +
          geom_hline(yintercept = min_cluster_size, linetype="dashed", color="red") +
          labs(y = "Minimum Cluster Size", x = "Number of Clusters (k)")
        p4 <- ggplot(ensemble_diag, aes(x = k, y = mean_richness)) +
          geom_line() + geom_point() +
          geom_hline(yintercept = min_mean_richness, linetype="dashed", color="blue") +
          labs(y = "Mean Species Richness per Cluster", x = "Number of Clusters (k)")
  
        print((p1 | p2) / (p3 | p4))
  
    # ---- Ensemble selection for plausible k ----
        recommended_k <- ensemble_diag$k[
          ensemble_diag$min_cluster_size >= min_cluster_size &
            ensemble_diag$mean_richness >= min_mean_richness &
            !is.na(ensemble_diag$between_jaccard)
        ]
        cat("\nPlausible cluster counts (k) w/o considering plots: ", paste(recommended_k, collapse=", "), "\n")
  
    # ---- Suggested k-value ----
        optimal_k <- 3
        cat("Suggested k for further analysis based on ensemble + plots:", optimal_k, "\n")

  # ==============================================================================
  # STEP 6: MIXING PARAMETER OPTIMIZATION (α)
  # ==============================================================================

    # Define grid of α values for evaluation
        alpha_grid <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.05)
  
    # Use ClustGeo's choicealpha function for systematic evaluation
        cat("Evaluating mixing parameter α across", length(alpha_grid), "values...\n")
  
        alpha_results <- ClustGeo::choicealpha(
            D0 = D0,
            D1 = D1,
            range.alpha = alpha_grid,
            K = optimal_k,
            graph = FALSE
        )

    # Extract and properly format the results
        alpha_df <- data.frame(
            alpha = alpha_grid,
            Q0 = alpha_results$Q[, "Q0"],
            Q1 = alpha_results$Q[, "Q1"],
            Q0_norm = alpha_results$Qnorm[, "Q0norm"],
            Q1_norm = alpha_results$Qnorm[, "Q1norm"]
        )
        
    # Find optimal α using multiple criteria
  
      # Method 1: Maximum product of normalized qualities
          alpha_df$product_norm <- alpha_df$Q0_norm * alpha_df$Q1_norm
    
      # Method 2: Minimum distance from ideal point (1,1) in normalized space
          alpha_df$distance_ideal <- sqrt((1 - alpha_df$Q0_norm)^2 + (1 - alpha_df$Q1_norm)^2)
  
    # Select optimal α
        alpha_product_opt <- alpha_grid[which.max(alpha_df$product_norm)]
        alpha_distance_opt <- alpha_grid[which.min(alpha_df$distance_ideal)]
        
        # Filter for viable alphas (ecological quality >= 90%, geographical quality >= 10%)
            viable_alphas <- alpha_df %>%
                dplyr::filter(
                    Q1_norm >= 0.1,    # At least 10% geographical improvement
                    Q0_norm >= 0.9     # No more than 10% ecological loss
                )
        
        if (nrow(viable_alphas) > 0) {
            optimal_alpha <- viable_alphas$alpha[which.max(viable_alphas$product_norm)]
        } else {
            optimal_alpha <- alpha_product_opt  # Fallback to product maximum
        }
  
        cat("Alpha optimization results:\n")
        cat("  Product optimization α =", alpha_product_opt, "\n")
        cat("  Distance optimization α =", alpha_distance_opt, "\n")
        cat("  Final selected α =", optimal_alpha, "\n")
  
    # Create comprehensive α selection visualization
        alpha_plot <- ggplot(alpha_df, aes(x = alpha)) +
            geom_line(aes(y = Q0_norm, color = "Ecological"), linewidth = 1) +
            geom_line(aes(y = Q1_norm, color = "Geographical"), linewidth = 1) +
            geom_vline(xintercept = optimal_alpha, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
            geom_point(
                data = alpha_df[alpha_df$alpha == optimal_alpha, ], 
                aes(y = Q0_norm), color = "red", size = 3
            ) +
            geom_point(
                data = alpha_df[alpha_df$alpha == optimal_alpha, ], 
                aes(y = Q1_norm), color = "red", size = 3
            ) +
            scale_color_manual(values = c("Ecological" = "#2E8B57", "Geographical" = "#4169E1")) +
            labs(
                title = "Mixing Parameter (α) Optimization",
                subtitle = paste(
                    "Optimal α =", optimal_alpha, 
                    "| Ecological =", round(alpha_df$Q0_norm[alpha_df$alpha == optimal_alpha], 3),
                    "| Geographical =", round(alpha_df$Q1_norm[alpha_df$alpha == optimal_alpha], 3)
                ),
                x = "Mixing Parameter (α)",
                y = "Normalized Quality (%)",
                color = "Homogeneity Type"
            ) +
            theme_minimal() +
            theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
        print(alpha_plot)

  # ==============================================================================
  # STEP 7: FINAL CLUSTERING WITH OPTIMAL PARAMETERS
  # ==============================================================================

    # Perform final clustering with optimal parameters
        final_tree <- ClustGeo::hclustgeo(D0, D1, alpha = optimal_alpha)
        final_clusters <- cutree(final_tree, k = optimal_k)
  
    # Create results summary dataframe
        results_summary <- data.frame(
            site_id = rownames(site_species_final),
            longitude = site_centroids_final$centroid_x,
            latitude = site_centroids_final$centroid_y,
            n_records = site_centroids_final$n_records,
            species_richness = rowSums(site_species_final),
            bioregion = final_clusters,
            stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
  
    # Convert to spatial object for mapping
        results_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(
            results_summary, 
            coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), 
            crs = sf::st_crs(combined_species_data)
        )
  
        cat("Final clustering summary:\n")
        cat("  Number of bioregions:", length(unique(final_clusters)), "\n")
        cat("  Sites per bioregion:", paste(table(final_clusters), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
    # Calculate bioregion summaries
        if (length(unique(final_clusters)) > 0 && !all(is.na(final_clusters))) {
            bioregion_summary <- results_summary %>%
                dplyr::group_by(bioregion) %>%
                dplyr::summarise(
                    n_sites = dplyr::n(),
                    total_records = sum(n_records),
                    mean_richness = round(mean(species_richness), 2),
                    min_richness = min(species_richness),
                    max_richness = max(species_richness),
                    .groups = 'drop'
                )
            
            print(bioregion_summary)
        } else {
            cat("Error: No valid bioregions created\n")
            bioregion_summary <- data.frame()
        }

  # ==============================================================================
  # STEP 8: VISUALIZATION AND VALIDATION
  # ==============================================================================

    # Create main bioregionalization map
        bioregion_map <- ggplot(results_sf) +
            geom_sf(aes(color = factor(bioregion), size = species_richness), alpha = 0.8) +
            scale_color_viridis_d(name = "Bioregion", option = "turbo") +
            scale_size_continuous(name = "Species\nRichness", range = c(1, 4)) +
            labs(
                title = "Tick Species Bioregions in Georgia",
                subtitle = paste0("ClustGeo method | K = ", optimal_k, ", α = ", optimal_alpha,
                                " | ", nrow(results_summary), " sites"),
                caption = "Spatially-constrained clustering balancing ecological and geographical similarity"
            ) +
            theme_minimal() +
            theme(
                axis.text = element_blank(),
                axis.ticks = element_blank(),
                panel.grid = element_blank()
            )
  
        print(bioregion_map)
  
    # Validate clustering quality
        if (length(unique(final_clusters)) > 1 && !all(is.na(final_clusters))) {
            final_sil_score <- mean(cluster::silhouette(final_clusters, D0)[,3])
            eco_homogeneity <- alpha_df$Q0[alpha_df$alpha == optimal_alpha]
            geo_homogeneity <- alpha_df$Q1[alpha_df$alpha == optimal_alpha]
        } else {
            final_sil_score <- NA
            eco_homogeneity <- NA
            geo_homogeneity <- NA
        }
  
        cat("\nClustering validation:\n")
        cat("  Silhouette score:", round(final_sil_score, 3), "\n")
        cat("  Ecological homogeneity:", round(eco_homogeneity, 3), "\n")
        cat("  Geographical homogeneity:", round(geo_homogeneity, 3), "\n")
  
    # Create bioregion composition analysis
        if (length(unique(final_clusters)) > 1 && !all(is.na(final_clusters))) {
            bioregion_composition <- purrr::map(1:optimal_k, function(region_id) {
                region_sites <- which(final_clusters == region_id)
                if (length(region_sites) > 0) {
                    species_prevalence <- colMeans(site_species_final[region_sites, , drop = FALSE])
                    species_prevalence[species_prevalence > 0]
                } else {
                    numeric(0)
                }
            }) %>%
                purrr::set_names(paste0("Region_", 1:optimal_k))
            
            cat("\nBioregion species composition (prevalence > 0):\n")
            purrr::iwalk(bioregion_composition, function(comp, region) {
                cat(region, ":", length(comp), "species\n")
                if (length(comp) > 0) {
                    cat("  Top species:", paste(names(sort(comp, decreasing = TRUE))[1:min(3, length(comp))], 
                                              collapse = ", "), "\n")
                }
            })
        } else {
            bioregion_composition <- list()
            cat("\nNo valid bioregions for composition analysis\n")
        }

  # ==============================================================================
  # STEP 9: EXPORT RESULTS (FIXED VERSION)
  # ==============================================================================

    # Create the missing clustering_metrics object
        clustering_metrics <- ensemble_diag

    # Create the cluster evaluation plot
        cluster_eval_plot <- (p1 | p2) / (p3 | p4)

    # Create comprehensive results object
        clustgeo_results <- list(
            # Input parameters
                parameters = list(
                    n_sites = nrow(site_species_final),
                    n_species = ncol(site_species_final),
                    optimal_k = optimal_k,
                    optimal_alpha = optimal_alpha,
                    method = "ClustGeo with Jaccard + Geographic distance"
                ),
            
            # Clustering results
                clustering = list(
                    bioregions = final_clusters,
                    tree = final_tree,
                    results_summary = results_summary,
                    results_sf = results_sf
                ),
            
            # Quality metrics
                validation = list(
                    silhouette_score = final_sil_score,
                    ecological_homogeneity = eco_homogeneity,
                    geographical_homogeneity = geo_homogeneity,
                    alpha_evaluation = alpha_df,
                    cluster_evaluation = clustering_metrics
                ),
            
            # Biological interpretation
                bioregions = list(
                    summary = bioregion_summary,
                    composition = bioregion_composition,
                    site_species_matrix = site_species_final
                ),
            
            # Dissimilarity matrices
                dissimilarities = list(
                    D0_ecological = D0,
                    D1_geographical = D1
                ),
            
            # Visualizations
                plots = list(
                    bioregion_map = bioregion_map,
                    alpha_optimization = alpha_plot,
                    cluster_evaluation = cluster_eval_plot
                )
        )

    # Save results
        assign("clustgeo_bioregionalization", clustgeo_results, envir = .GlobalEnv)

        cat("Results saved to 'clustgeo_bioregionalization' object\n")
        cat("\nClustGeo bioregionalization completed successfully!\n")
        cat("================================================================================\n")

    # Print final summary
        cat("\nFINAL SUMMARY:\n")
        cat("Method: ClustGeo spatially-constrained hierarchical clustering\n")
        cat("Sites analyzed:", nrow(site_species_final), "\n")
        cat("Species included:", ncol(site_species_final), "\n") 
        cat("Bioregions identified:", optimal_k, "\n")
        cat("Mixing parameter α:", optimal_alpha, "\n")
        cat("Ecological vs Geographical balance:", round(eco_homogeneity, 3), "vs", round(geo_homogeneity, 3), "\n")
        cat("Overall clustering quality (silhouette):", round(final_sil_score, 3), "\n")

```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 15. BIOREGIONALIZATION ANALYTIC
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r BIOREGIONALIZATION ANALYTICS}

# ==============================================================================
# BIOREGIONALIZATION DETAILED ANALYSIS
# ==============================================================================

    # Extract key objects from clustgeo results
        clustgeo_results <- get("clustgeo_bioregionalization")
        results_summary <- clustgeo_results$clustering$results_summary
        site_species_final <- clustgeo_results$bioregions$site_species_matrix
        final_clusters <- clustgeo_results$clustering$bioregions
        env_data_final <- get("env_data_final")  # From the original analysis

    # --- ENVIRONMENTAL CHARACTERIZATION OF BIOREGIONS ---
    
        # Calculate environmental means and ranges for each bioregion
            env_bioregion_stats <- purrr::map_dfr(1:max(final_clusters), function(region_id) {
                region_sites <- which(final_clusters == region_id)
                region_env <- env_data_final[region_sites, , drop = FALSE]
                
                # Calculate statistics for key environmental variables
                    key_vars <- c("Annual_Mean_Temperature", "Annual_Precipitation", "Elevation", 
                                 "NDVI_mean", "SoilpH", "landscape_diversity_shdi")
                    available_vars <- intersect(key_vars, colnames(region_env))
                
                # Create summary for available variables
                    env_summary <- purrr::map_dfr(available_vars, function(var) {
                        values <- region_env[, var]
                        tibble::tibble(
                            bioregion = region_id,
                            variable = var,
                            mean_value = round(mean(values, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
                            sd_value = round(sd(values, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
                            min_value = round(min(values, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
                            max_value = round(max(values, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
                            range_text = paste0(min_value, "-", max_value)
                        )
                    })
                
                return(env_summary)
            })
        
        # Pivot to wider format for easier interpretation
            env_by_region <- env_bioregion_stats %>%
                dplyr::select(bioregion, variable, mean_value) %>%
                tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = variable, values_from = mean_value, 
                                  names_prefix = "mean_")

    # --- STATISTICAL INDICATOR SPECIES ANALYSIS USING INDICSPECIES ---
    
        # Prepare data for indicspecies analysis
            # Create cluster vector for sites
                cluster_vector <- final_clusters
                names(cluster_vector) <- rownames(site_species_final)
        
        # Run IndVal analysis using literature-recommended parameters
            # Using IndVal.g (group-equalized) method with 9999 permutations for robust results
                indval_results <- indicspecies::multipatt(
                    site_species_final,             # Sites x species matrix (standard format)
                    cluster = cluster_vector,       # Bioregion assignments
                    func = "IndVal.g",              # Group-equalized IndVal (recommended)
                    duleg = TRUE,                   # Include Dufrene-Legendre components
                    control = how(nperm = 9999)     # High permutation count for robust p-values
                )
        
        # Extract and format indicator species results
            # Extract from the sign matrix which contains significance results
            
        # Create comprehensive indicator species dataframe
            indicator_species_results <- purrr::map_dfr(1:max(final_clusters), function(region_id) {
                # Get species that are significant indicators for this region
                    region_col <- paste0("s.", region_id)
                    
                # Find species with significant associations to this region
                    significant_species <- rownames(indval_results$sign)[
                        indval_results$sign[, region_col] == 1 & 
                        indval_results$sign[, "p.value"] <= 0.1
                    ]
                
                if (length(significant_species) > 0) {
                    # Extract statistics for these species
                        indval_scores <- indval_results$sign[significant_species, "stat"]
                        p_values <- indval_results$sign[significant_species, "p.value"]
                        specificity <- indval_results$A[significant_species, region_id]
                        fidelity <- indval_results$B[significant_species, region_id]
                    
                    tibble::tibble(
                        bioregion = region_id,
                        species = significant_species,
                        indval_score = round(indval_scores * 100, 2),  # Convert to percentage
                        p_value = round(p_values, 4),
                        specificity = round(specificity, 3),
                        fidelity = round(fidelity, 3),
                        significance = case_when(
                            p_values <= 0.001 ~ "***",
                            p_values <= 0.01 ~ "**", 
                            p_values <= 0.05 ~ "*",
                            p_values <= 0.1 ~ ".",
                            TRUE ~ ""
                        ),
                        strength = case_when(
                            indval_scores * 100 >= 50 ~ "Highly diagnostic",
                            indval_scores * 100 >= 25 ~ "Strong indicator",
                            indval_scores * 100 >= 10 ~ "Moderate indicator",
                            TRUE ~ "Weak indicator"
                        )
                    ) %>%
                        dplyr::arrange(desc(indval_score))
                } else {
                    # No species for this region
                        tibble::tibble(
                            bioregion = numeric(0),
                            species = character(0),
                            indval_score = numeric(0),
                            p_value = numeric(0),
                            specificity = numeric(0),
                            fidelity = numeric(0),
                            significance = character(0),
                            strength = character(0)
                        )
                }
            })
        
        # Apply FDR correction across all tests (Benjamini-Hochberg)
            if (nrow(indicator_species_results) > 0) {
                indicator_species_results$p_adj <- p.adjust(indicator_species_results$p_value, method = "BH")
                indicator_species_results$adj_significance <- case_when(
                    indicator_species_results$p_adj <= 0.001 ~ "***",
                    indicator_species_results$p_adj <= 0.01 ~ "**",
                    indicator_species_results$p_adj <= 0.05 ~ "*",
                    indicator_species_results$p_adj <= 0.1 ~ ".",
                    TRUE ~ ""
                )
            }

    # --- SPECIES COMPOSITION AND PREVALENCE ANALYSIS ---
    
        # Calculate species prevalence within each bioregion for context
            species_by_region <- purrr::map(1:max(final_clusters), function(region_id) {
                region_sites <- which(final_clusters == region_id)
                region_matrix <- site_species_final[region_sites, , drop = FALSE]
                
                # Calculate prevalence (proportion of sites where species occurs)
                    prevalence <- colMeans(region_matrix) * 100  # Convert to percentage
                    
                # Filter to species that occur in this region
                    present_species <- prevalence[prevalence > 0]
                    
                tibble::tibble(
                    bioregion = region_id,
                    species = names(present_species),
                    prevalence = round(as.numeric(present_species), 1),
                    n_sites_present = round(prevalence * length(region_sites) / 100)
                ) %>%
                    dplyr::arrange(desc(prevalence))
            }) %>%
                purrr::set_names(paste0("Region_", 1:max(final_clusters)))

    # --- BIODIVERSITY CALCULATIONS BY BIOREGION ---
    
        # Get the correct occurrence point counts from the original bioregion summary
            original_bioregion_summary <- clustgeo_results$bioregions$summary
    
        # Calculate proper diversity metrics for each bioregion
            diversity_by_region <- purrr::map_dfr(1:max(final_clusters), function(region_id) {
                region_sites <- which(final_clusters == region_id)
                region_matrix <- site_species_final[region_sites, , drop = FALSE]
                
                # Get correct occurrence points from original summary
                    region_occurrence_points <- original_bioregion_summary$total_records[
                        original_bioregion_summary$bioregion == region_id
                    ]
                
                # Calculate species occurrence counts across all sites in region
                    species_counts <- colSums(region_matrix)
                    
                # Remove species not present in this region
                    present_counts <- species_counts[species_counts > 0]
                    
                # Calculate proportional abundance for each species
                    prop_abundance <- present_counts / sum(present_counts)
                
                # Basic measures
                    S <- length(present_counts)  # Species richness
                    total_abundance <- sum(present_counts)
                
                # Shannon diversity components
                    shannon_components <- ifelse(prop_abundance > 0, 
                                               -prop_abundance * log(prop_abundance), 0)
                    shannon_diversity <- sum(shannon_components)
                    shannon_evenness <- shannon_diversity / log(S)
                
                # Simpson diversity components
                    simpson_components <- prop_abundance^2
                    simpson_dominance <- sum(simpson_components)      # Original Simpson λ
                    gini_simpson <- 1 - simpson_dominance             # Gini-Simpson (1-λ)
                    simpson_evenness <- (1 / simpson_dominance) / S
                
                # Dominance measures
                    berger_parker <- max(prop_abundance)
                
                # Hill numbers (effective species diversity)
                    hill_q0 <- S                                      # Species richness
                    hill_q1 <- exp(shannon_diversity)                # Shannon effective species
                    hill_q2 <- 1 / simpson_dominance                 # Simpson effective species
                
                # Mean richness per site in this region
                    mean_site_richness <- mean(rowSums(region_matrix))
                
                tibble::tibble(
                    bioregion = region_id,
                    n_sites = length(region_sites),
                    n_occurrence_points = region_occurrence_points,
                    total_species = S,
                    total_abundance = total_abundance,
                    mean_richness_per_site = round(mean_site_richness, 2),
                    shannon_diversity = round(shannon_diversity, 3),
                    shannon_evenness = round(shannon_evenness, 3),
                    simpson_dominance = round(simpson_dominance, 3),
                    gini_simpson = round(gini_simpson, 3),
                    simpson_evenness = round(simpson_evenness, 3),
                    berger_parker = round(berger_parker, 3),
                    hill_q0 = hill_q0,
                    hill_q1 = round(hill_q1, 2),
                    hill_q2 = round(hill_q2, 2)
                )
            })

    # --- STATISTICAL TESTS OF BIODIVERSITY DIFFERENCES ---
        
        # Create site-level diversity data for statistical testing
            site_diversity_data <- purrr::map_dfr(1:nrow(site_species_final), function(i) {
                site_species <- site_species_final[i, ]
                present_species <- site_species[site_species > 0]
                
                if (length(present_species) > 0) {
                    prop_abundance <- present_species / sum(present_species)
                    S_site <- length(present_species)
                    
                    # Calculate diversity metrics for this site
                        shannon_site <- -sum(prop_abundance * log(prop_abundance))
                        simpson_site <- 1 - sum(prop_abundance^2)  # Using Gini-Simpson
                        
                    tibble::tibble(
                        site_id = rownames(site_species_final)[i],
                        bioregion = as.factor(final_clusters[i]),
                        richness = S_site,
                        shannon = shannon_site,
                        simpson = simpson_site
                    )
                } else {
                    tibble::tibble(
                        site_id = rownames(site_species_final)[i],
                        bioregion = as.factor(final_clusters[i]),
                        richness = 0,
                        shannon = 0,
                        simpson = 0
                    )
                }
            })
        
        # Perform comprehensive statistical tests for each diversity metric
            diversity_statistical_tests <- purrr::map_dfr(c("richness", "shannon", "simpson", "hill_q1", "hill_q2"), function(metric) {
                # Determine the column name for Hill numbers
                    if (metric == "hill_q1") {
                        metric_data <- diversity_by_region$hill_q1[match(site_diversity_data$bioregion, diversity_by_region$bioregion)]
                        site_diversity_data$hill_q1 <- metric_data
                        formula_str <- "hill_q1 ~ bioregion"
                    } else if (metric == "hill_q2") {
                        metric_data <- diversity_by_region$hill_q2[match(site_diversity_data$bioregion, diversity_by_region$bioregion)]
                        site_diversity_data$hill_q2 <- metric_data
                        formula_str <- "hill_q2 ~ bioregion"
                    } else {
                        formula_str <- paste(metric, "~ bioregion")
                    }
                
                # ANOVA test
                    aov_result <- aov(as.formula(formula_str), data = site_diversity_data)
                    aov_summary <- summary(aov_result)
                    
                    f_stat <- aov_summary[[1]]$`F value`[1]
                    p_value <- aov_summary[[1]]$`Pr(>F)`[1]
                    df_between <- aov_summary[[1]]$Df[1]
                    df_within <- aov_summary[[1]]$Df[2]
                
                # Post-hoc Tukey HSD test if ANOVA is significant
                    tukey_result <- NULL
                    if (p_value < 0.05) {
                        tukey_result <- TukeyHSD(aov_result)
                        significant_pairs <- sum(tukey_result$bioregion[, "p adj"] < 0.05)
                        total_pairs <- nrow(tukey_result$bioregion)
                    } else {
                        significant_pairs <- 0
                        total_pairs <- choose(length(unique(site_diversity_data$bioregion)), 2)
                    }
                
                # Kruskal-Wallis non-parametric test
                    kw_result <- kruskal.test(as.formula(formula_str), data = site_diversity_data)
                    kw_statistic <- kw_result$statistic
                    kw_p_value <- kw_result$p.value
                
                # Effect size calculation (eta-squared)
                    ss_between <- aov_summary[[1]]$`Sum Sq`[1]
                    ss_total <- sum(aov_summary[[1]]$`Sum Sq`)
                    eta_squared <- ss_between / ss_total
                
                tibble::tibble(
                    diversity_metric = metric,
                    # ANOVA results
                    f_statistic = round(f_stat, 3),
                    anova_p_value = round(p_value, 4),
                    df_between = df_between,
                    df_within = df_within,
                    eta_squared = round(eta_squared, 3),
                    # Kruskal-Wallis results
                    kw_statistic = round(kw_statistic, 3),
                    kw_p_value = round(kw_p_value, 4),
                    # Post-hoc results
                    significant_pairs = significant_pairs,
                    total_pairs = total_pairs,
                    # Interpretation
                    anova_significant = p_value < 0.05,
                    kw_significant = kw_p_value < 0.05,
                    effect_size = case_when(
                        eta_squared >= 0.14 ~ "Large",
                        eta_squared >= 0.06 ~ "Medium", 
                        eta_squared >= 0.01 ~ "Small",
                        TRUE ~ "Negligible"
                    )
                )
            })

    # --- ENVIRONMENTAL DISTINCTIVENESS ANALYSIS ---
    
        # Identify which environmental variables best separate the regions
            env_separation <- purrr::map_dfr(colnames(env_data_final), function(var) {
                # Test ANOVA across bioregions
                    var_data <- data.frame(
                        env_value = env_data_final[, var],
                        bioregion = as.factor(final_clusters)
                    )
                
                # Perform ANOVA
                    aov_result <- tryCatch({
                        aov(env_value ~ bioregion, data = var_data)
                    }, error = function(e) NULL)
                
                if (!is.null(aov_result)) {
                    aov_summary <- summary(aov_result)
                    f_stat <- aov_summary[[1]]$`F value`[1]
                    p_value <- aov_summary[[1]]$`Pr(>F)`[1]
                    
                    # Calculate effect size
                        ss_between <- aov_summary[[1]]$`Sum Sq`[1]
                        ss_total <- sum(aov_summary[[1]]$`Sum Sq`)
                        eta_squared <- ss_between / ss_total
                    
                    # Calculate between-group variance
                        group_means <- tapply(var_data$env_value, var_data$bioregion, mean, na.rm = TRUE)
                        between_var <- var(group_means, na.rm = TRUE)
                    
                    tibble::tibble(
                        variable = var,
                        f_statistic = round(f_stat, 3),
                        p_value = round(p_value, 4),
                        eta_squared = round(eta_squared, 3),
                        between_variance = round(between_var, 3),
                        significant = p_value < 0.05,
                        effect_size = case_when(
                            eta_squared >= 0.14 ~ "Large",
                            eta_squared >= 0.06 ~ "Medium",
                            eta_squared >= 0.01 ~ "Small",
                            TRUE ~ "Negligible"
                        )
                    )
                } else {
                    tibble::tibble(
                        variable = var,
                        f_statistic = NA,
                        p_value = NA,
                        eta_squared = NA,
                        between_variance = NA,
                        significant = FALSE,
                        effect_size = "Error"
                    )
                }
            }) %>%
                dplyr::filter(!is.na(f_statistic)) %>%
                dplyr::arrange(desc(f_statistic))

    # --- COMPILE COMPREHENSIVE RESULTS ---
    
        bioregion_detailed_analysis <- list(
            environmental_characterization = list(
                by_region_summary = env_by_region,
                detailed_stats = env_bioregion_stats,
                separation_analysis = env_separation
            ),
            species_composition = list(
                by_region = species_by_region,
                indicator_species_statistical = indicator_species_results,
                indval_full_results = indval_results
            ),
            diversity_analysis = list(
                regional_diversity = diversity_by_region,
                site_level_data = site_diversity_data,
                statistical_tests = diversity_statistical_tests
            ),
            bioregion_summary = clustgeo_results$bioregions$summary
        )
    
    # Export to global environment
        assign("bioregion_detailed_analysis", bioregion_detailed_analysis, envir = .GlobalEnv)

    # --- ENHANCED SUMMARY STATISTICS FOR MANUSCRIPT ---
    
        cat("=== ENHANCED BIOREGIONALIZATION ANALYSIS ===\n\n")
        
        cat("1. STATISTICAL INDICATOR SPECIES ANALYSIS (IndVal.g method, n=9999 permutations)\n")
        cat("----------------------------------------------------------------\n")
        if (nrow(indicator_species_results) > 0) {
            # Count indicators by strength and significance
                strong_indicators <- sum(indicator_species_results$indval_score >= 25 & 
                                       indicator_species_results$p_adj <= 0.05)
                total_indicators <- nrow(indicator_species_results %>% 
                                       dplyr::filter(p_adj <= 0.05))
                
            cat("Total significant indicators (FDR ≤ 0.05):", total_indicators, "\n")
            cat("Strong indicators (IndVal ≥ 25%):", strong_indicators, "\n\n")
            
            # Display top indicators by region
                purrr::walk(1:max(final_clusters), function(region_id) {
                    region_indicators <- indicator_species_results %>% 
                        dplyr::filter(bioregion == region_id, p_adj <= 0.05) %>%
                        head(3)
                    
                    if (nrow(region_indicators) > 0) {
                        cat("Region", region_id, "top indicators:\n")
                        purrr::pwalk(list(region_indicators$species, 
                                        region_indicators$indval_score,
                                        region_indicators$adj_significance,
                                        region_indicators$strength),
                                   function(sp, score, sig, strength) {
                                       cat("  ", sp, ": IndVal =", score, "% ", sig, " (", strength, ")\n")
                                   })
                    } else {
                        cat("Region", region_id, ": No significant indicators\n")
                    }
                })
        } else {
            cat("No significant indicator species found\n")
        }
        cat("\n")
        
        cat("2. BIODIVERSITY STATISTICAL COMPARISONS\n")
        cat("----------------------------------------\n")
        purrr::pwalk(list(diversity_statistical_tests$diversity_metric, 
                         diversity_statistical_tests$f_statistic,
                         diversity_statistical_tests$anova_p_value,
                         diversity_statistical_tests$eta_squared,
                         diversity_statistical_tests$effect_size,
                         diversity_statistical_tests$significant_pairs,
                         diversity_statistical_tests$total_pairs,
                         diversity_statistical_tests$anova_significant), 
                    function(metric, f_stat, p_val, eta, effect, sig_pairs, total_pairs, is_sig) {
                        sig_text <- ifelse(p_val < 0.001, "***", 
                                         ifelse(p_val < 0.01, "**",
                                               ifelse(p_val < 0.05, "*", "")))
                        cat(stringr::str_to_title(metric), "diversity:\n")
                        cat("  ANOVA: F =", f_stat, ", p =", p_val, sig_text, 
                            " (η² =", eta, ",", effect, "effect)\n")
                        if (is_sig) {
                            cat("  Post-hoc:", sig_pairs, "of", total_pairs, "pairwise comparisons significant\n")
                        } else {
                            cat("  Post-hoc: Not performed (ANOVA not significant)\n")
                        }
                    })
        cat("\n")
        
        cat("3. ENVIRONMENTAL SEPARATION ANALYSIS\n")
        cat("------------------------------------\n")
        cat("Top environmental variables separating bioregions:\n")
        top_env_vars <- head(env_separation %>% dplyr::filter(significant == TRUE), 5)
        if (nrow(top_env_vars) > 0) {
            purrr::pwalk(list(top_env_vars$variable, 
                             top_env_vars$f_statistic, 
                             top_env_vars$eta_squared,
                             top_env_vars$effect_size), 
                        function(var, f_stat, eta, effect) {
                            cat("  ", var, ": F =", f_stat, " (η² =", eta, ",", effect, "effect)\n")
                        })
        } else {
            cat("  No environmental variables significantly separate bioregions\n")
        }
        cat("\n")
        
        cat("4. BIOREGION DIVERSITY SUMMARY\n")
        cat("------------------------------\n")
        purrr::pwalk(list(diversity_by_region$bioregion, 
                         diversity_by_region$n_sites,
                         diversity_by_region$n_occurrence_points,
                         diversity_by_region$total_species, 
                         diversity_by_region$shannon_diversity,
                         diversity_by_region$gini_simpson,
                         diversity_by_region$hill_q1,
                         diversity_by_region$hill_q2), 
                    function(region, n_sites, n_points, total, shannon, simpson, hill1, hill2) {
                        cat("Region", region, "(", n_sites, "sites,", n_points, "occurrence points):\n")
                        cat("  ", total, "species | Shannon =", shannon, "| Simpson =", simpson, "\n")
                        cat("  Hill q1 =", hill1, "| Hill q2 =", hill2, "\n")
                    })
        cat("\n")
        
        cat("5. ENVIRONMENTAL CHARACTERIZATION (KEY VARIABLES)\n")
        cat("-------------------------------------------------\n")
        key_summary_vars <- c("Annual_Mean_Temperature", "Annual_Precipitation", "Elevation")
        available_summary_vars <- intersect(key_summary_vars, env_separation$variable)
        
        purrr::walk(available_summary_vars, function(var) {
            var_stats <- env_bioregion_stats %>% dplyr::filter(variable == var)
            var_test <- env_separation %>% dplyr::filter(variable == var)
            cat(var, " (F =", var_test$f_statistic, ", p =", var_test$p_value, "):\n")
            purrr::pwalk(list(var_stats$bioregion, var_stats$mean_value, var_stats$range_text),
                        function(region, mean_val, range_val) {
                            cat("  Region", region, ": mean =", mean_val, ", range =", range_val, "\n")
                        })
        })
        
        cat("\n=== ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n")
        
```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 16. BIOREGIONALIZATION THIESSEN MAP
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r BIOREGIONALIZATION THIESSEN MAP}
# ==============================================================================
# STANDALONE DELDIR BIOREGIONALIZATION MAP CREATION - TWO VERSIONS
# ==============================================================================

   # Load required packages
       library(deldir)
       library(dplyr)
       library(sf)
       library(ggplot2)
       library(viridis)

   # Transform to projected coordinate system for accurate calculations
       results_sf_proj <- sf::st_transform(results_sf, crs = 32638)  # UTM Zone 38N
       georgia_boundary_proj <- sf::st_transform(Georgia_Boundary, crs = 32638)
   
   # Extract coordinates and site information
       coords_proj <- sf::st_coordinates(results_sf_proj)
       site_coords_df <- data.frame(
           x = coords_proj[, "X"],
           y = coords_proj[, "Y"],
           site_id = results_summary$site_id,
           bioregion = results_summary$bioregion,
           species_richness = results_summary$species_richness
       )
   
   # Get Georgia boundary box for Thiessen polygon creation
       georgia_bbox_proj <- sf::st_bbox(georgia_boundary_proj)
   
   # Create Thiessen polygons using deldir
       thiessen_deldir <- deldir::deldir(
           x = site_coords_df$x,
           y = site_coords_df$y,
           rw = c(georgia_bbox_proj$xmin, georgia_bbox_proj$xmax, 
                  georgia_bbox_proj$ymin, georgia_bbox_proj$ymax),
           suppressMsge = TRUE
       )
   
   # Convert deldir tiles to sf polygons
       thiessen_tiles <- deldir::tile.list(thiessen_deldir)
       
       tile_to_polygon <- function(tile) {
           if (length(tile$x) < 3) return(NULL)
           
           coords_matrix <- matrix(c(c(tile$x, tile$x[1]), 
                                   c(tile$y, tile$y[1])), ncol = 2)
           poly <- sf::st_polygon(list(coords_matrix))
           return(poly)
       }
       
       polygon_list <- purrr::map(thiessen_tiles, tile_to_polygon) %>%
           purrr::compact()
       
       thiessen_sf <- sf::st_sf(
           tile_id = 1:length(polygon_list),
           geometry = sf::st_sfc(polygon_list, crs = 32638)
       )
   
   # Add bioregion information
       thiessen_with_bioregions <- thiessen_sf %>%
           dplyr::mutate(
               site_id = site_coords_df$site_id,
               bioregion = site_coords_df$bioregion,
               species_richness = site_coords_df$species_richness
           )
   
   # Clip to Georgia boundary
       thiessen_clipped <- sf::st_intersection(thiessen_with_bioregions, georgia_boundary_proj)
   
   # Dissolve polygons by bioregion
       bioregion_polygons_proj <- thiessen_clipped %>%
           dplyr::group_by(bioregion) %>%
           dplyr::summarise(
               n_polygons = dplyr::n(),
               .groups = 'drop'
           )
   
   # Transform back to WGS84
       bioregion_polygons <- sf::st_transform(bioregion_polygons_proj, crs = 4326)
       thiessen_clipped_wgs84 <- sf::st_transform(thiessen_clipped, crs = 4326)
   
   # VERSION 1: Clean dissolved regions map
       bioregion_map_clean <- ggplot() +
           geom_sf(
               data = bioregion_polygons,
               aes(fill = factor(bioregion)),
               alpha = 0.8,
               color = "white",
               linewidth = 1.5
           ) +
           geom_sf(
               data = Georgia_Boundary,
               fill = NA,
               color = "black",
               linewidth = 2
           ) +
           geom_sf(
               data = results_sf,
               aes(size = species_richness),
               color = "black",
               alpha = 0.9
           ) +
           scale_fill_viridis_d(
               name = "Bioregion",
               option = "turbo",
               begin = 0.1,
               end = 0.9,
               guide = "none"
           ) +
           scale_size_continuous(
               name = "Species Richness",
               range = c(1, 4),
               guide = "none"
           ) +
           theme_void() +
           theme(
               panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
               plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)#,
               #plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0)
           )
   
   # VERSION 2: Map with Thiessen polygon boundaries visible
       
        jenks_breaks <- classIntervals(
            results_sf$species_richness,
            n = 3,
            style = "jenks"
        )
        
        # Create a factor variable with Jenks break categories
          results_sf <- results_sf |>
              dplyr::mutate(
                  richness_class = cut(
                      species_richness,
                      breaks = jenks_breaks$brks,
                      include.lowest = TRUE,
                      labels = FALSE
                  )
              )

          bioregion_map_thiessen <- ggplot() +
              geom_sf(
                  data = thiessen_clipped_wgs84,
                  aes(fill = factor(bioregion)),
                  alpha = 0.7,
                  color = "white",
                  linewidth = 0.3
              ) +
              geom_sf(
                  data = bioregion_polygons,
                  fill = NA,
                  color = "black",
                  linewidth = 1.5
              ) +
              geom_sf(
                  data = Georgia_Boundary,
                  fill = NA,
                  color = "black",
                  linewidth = 2
              ) +
              geom_sf(
                  data = results_sf,
                  aes(size = factor(richness_class)),
                  color = "black",
                  alpha = 0.9
              ) +
              scale_fill_viridis_d(
                  name = "Bioregion",
                  option = "turbo",
                  begin = 0.1,
                  end = 0.9,
                  guide = "none"
              ) +
              scale_size_manual(
                  name = "Species Richness",
                  values = c(2, 4.5, 7),
                  guide = "none"
              ) +
              theme_void() +
              theme(
                  panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
                  plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
              )
   
   # Create standalone legend for bioregions
       legend_bioregion <- ggplot() +
           geom_sf(
               data = bioregion_polygons,
               aes(fill = factor(bioregion)),
               alpha = 0.8
           ) +
           scale_fill_viridis_d(
               name = "Bioregion",
               option = "turbo",
               begin = 0.1,
               end = 0.9,
               labels = paste("Region", 1:3)
           ) +
           theme_void() +
           theme(
               legend.position = "bottom",
               legend.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
               legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
               legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm"),
               panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
               plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
           ) +
           guides(
               fill = guide_legend(
                   title.position = "top",
                   title.hjust = 0.5,
                   override.aes = list(alpha = 1)
               )
           )
   
   # Create standalone legend for species richness
       legend_richness <- ggplot() +
           geom_sf(
               data = results_sf,
               aes(size = species_richness),
               color = "black",
               alpha = 0.9
           ) +
           scale_size_continuous(
               name = "Species Richness",
               range = c(1, 4),
               breaks = c(1, 3, 5, 7),
               labels = c("1", "3", "5", "7")
           ) +
           theme_void() +
           theme(
               legend.position = "bottom",
               legend.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
               legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
               panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
               plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
           ) +
           guides(
               size = guide_legend(
                   title.position = "top",
                   title.hjust = 0.5,
                   override.aes = list(alpha = 1)
               )
           )
   
   # Display both maps
       print(bioregion_map_clean)
       print(bioregion_map_thiessen)
```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 17. RAREFACTION
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r RAREFACTION}

  # --- RAREFACTION & ESTIMATORS ---
  
    # Retrieve site-species matrix
      site_species_matrix <- site_species_final
    
    # Inspect dimensions and column names
      cat("Matrix dimensions:", dim(site_species_matrix), "\n")
      cat("Species columns:\n")
      print(colnames(site_species_matrix))
    
    # Ensure matrix is in proper format
      site_species_matrix <- as.data.frame(site_species_matrix)
    
    # Calculate Chao1 and Jackknife Estimators
      chao1_estimate <- estimateR(t(site_species_matrix))
      jack1_estimate <- specpool(site_species_matrix, pool = rep("all", nrow(site_species_matrix)))
    
    # Compute mean Chao1 estimate
      mean_chao1 <- mean(chao1_estimate["S.chao1", ], na.rm = TRUE)
    
    # Prepare incidence data for iNEXT (single assemblage)
      inext_input <- c(nrow(site_species_matrix), colSums(site_species_matrix))
      inext_input <- inext_input[inext_input > 0]  # remove species never observed
      inext_result <- iNEXT(inext_input, q = 0, datatype = "incidence_freq")
    
    # Plot accumulation curve and overlay estimates
      specacc <- specaccum(site_species_matrix, method = "random")
    
    # Plot accumulation
      plot(specacc, ci.type = "polygon", col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2, ci.lty = 0, ci.col = "lightgreen")
      legend("bottomright", legend = c("Accumulation", "Chao1", "Jackknife1"), col = c("darkgreen", "blue", "purple"), pch = c(NA, 16, 17), lty = c(1, NA, NA))
    
    # Plot iNEXT (single assemblage)
      print(
        ggiNEXT(inext_result, type = 1) +
          theme_minimal() +
          ggtitle("iNEXT Species Accumulation Curve")
      )
    
    # Output estimates
      list(
        Chao1 = chao1_estimate,
        Jackknife1 = jack1_estimate,
        iNEXT_summary = inext_result$AsyEst
      )


```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 18. NETWORK ANALYSIS
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r NETWORK ANALYSIS}

# ==============================================================================
# VEECH CO-OCCURRENCE NETWORK
# ==============================================================================

    # Transpose and format for cooccur
        cooc_input <- t(site_species_matrix)
    
    # Assign cleaned species names
        rownames(cooc_input) <- gsub("_", " ", rownames(cooc_input))
    
    # Run cooccur analysis
        co <- cooccur(mat = cooc_input, spp_names = TRUE)
        co_table <- as.data.frame(print(co))
    
    # Define nodes for Veech network
        veech_nodes <- data.frame(
            id = 1:nrow(cooc_input),
            label = rownames(cooc_input),
            color = "grey",
            shadow = TRUE
        )
    
    # Define edges for Veech network
        veech_edges <- data.frame(
            from = co_table$sp1,
            to = co_table$sp2,
            width = 1,
            color = ifelse(
                co_table$p_lt <= 0.05, "red",          # Red = significant negative association
                ifelse(co_table$p_gt <= 0.05, "blue", "green")  # Blue = significant positive, Green = neutral
            )
        )
    
    # Visualize Veech network
        veech_network <- visNetwork(nodes = veech_nodes, edges = veech_edges) %>%
            visIgraphLayout(layout = "layout_with_kk") %>%
            visNodes(size = 15) %>%
            visEdges(arrows = "none") %>%
            visOptions(highlightNearest = TRUE)
    
    # Display Veech network
        print(veech_network)


# ==============================================================================
# COUNT CO-OCCURRENCE NETWORK ANALYSIS
# ==============================================================================
        
    # Ensure binary matrix (presence/absence)
        site_species_binary <- site_species_final
        site_species_binary[site_species_binary > 1] <- 1
    
    # Clean species names for display
        colnames(site_species_binary) <- gsub("_", " ", colnames(site_species_binary))
    
    # Calculate co-occurrence counts between all species pairs
        cooccur_counts <- site_species_binary %>%
            as.matrix() %>%
            t() %>%                                     # Transpose to species × sites
            tcrossprod()                                # Matrix multiplication gives co-occurrence counts
        
    # Set diagonal to 0 (species don't co-occur with themselves for edge purposes)
        diag(cooccur_counts) <- 0
    
    # Calculate site occurrence counts for each species (node sizes)
        site_counts <- colSums(site_species_binary)
    
    # Create adjacency matrix for network (only keep edges with co-occurrence > threshold)
        cooccur_threshold <- 1                          # Minimum co-occurrences to show edge
        adj_matrix <- cooccur_counts
        adj_matrix[adj_matrix < cooccur_threshold] <- 0
    
    # Create igraph object
        cooccur_graph <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(
            adj_matrix, 
            mode = "undirected", 
            weighted = TRUE, 
            diag = FALSE
        )

    # Function to extract genus and species for sorting
        extract_genus_species <- function(species_names) {
            parsed <- stringr::str_split(species_names, " ", simplify = TRUE)
            data.frame(
                full_name = species_names,
                genus = parsed[, 1],
                species = parsed[, 2],
                stringsAsFactors = FALSE
            )
        }

    # Create alphabetical ordering
        species_df <- extract_genus_species(names(site_counts))
        alphabetical_order <- species_df %>%
            dplyr::arrange(genus, species) %>%
            dplyr::pull(full_name)

    # Create optimal layout function
        create_optimal_layout <- function(species_order, radius = 2.5) {
            n_nodes <- length(species_order)
            order_indices <- match(species_order, V(cooccur_graph)$name)
            
            angles <- seq(0, 2*pi, length.out = n_nodes + 1)[1:n_nodes]
```
