```{r NETWORK ANALYSIS}
            layout_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = vcount(cooccur_graph), ncol = 2)
            layout_matrix[order_indices, 1] <- radius * cos(angles)
            layout_matrix[order_indices, 2] <- radius * sin(angles)
            
            return(layout_matrix)
        }

    # Create layout
        optimal_alpha_layout <- create_optimal_layout(alphabetical_order, radius = 2.5)

    # Set vertex attributes
        V(cooccur_graph)$name <- names(site_counts)
        V(cooccur_graph)$site_count <- site_counts[V(cooccur_graph)$name]
        V(cooccur_graph)$size <- scales::rescale(site_counts[V(cooccur_graph)$name], to = c(6, 20))
        V(cooccur_graph)$label.cex <- 0.7
        V(cooccur_graph)$label.color <- "black"
        V(cooccur_graph)$frame.color <- "white"
        V(cooccur_graph)$frame.width <- 1.5

    # Set edge attributes
        edge_weights <- E(cooccur_graph)$weight
        E(cooccur_graph)$weight_original <- edge_weights
        E(cooccur_graph)$width <- scales::rescale(edge_weights, to = c(1.5, 8))

    # Create plotting function with no title
        create_subtle_green_plot <- function(layout_matrix, species_order) {
            # Simpler green scheme
                V(cooccur_graph)$color <- colorRampPalette(c("#9cdf8d", "#3541A5"))(max(site_counts))[site_counts[V(cooccur_graph)$name]]
                E(cooccur_graph)$color <- scales::alpha(colorRampPalette(c("#9cdf8d", "#3541A5"))(max(edge_weights))[edge_weights], 0.6)
            
            label_angles <- atan2(layout_matrix[, 2], layout_matrix[, 1])
            label_degrees <- label_angles * 180 / pi
            adjusted_angles <- ifelse(abs(label_degrees) > 90, label_degrees + 180, label_degrees)
            
            par(mar = c(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5))            # Removed title margin
            
            plot(cooccur_graph,
                 layout = layout_matrix,
                 vertex.label = V(cooccur_graph)$name,
                 vertex.label.dist = 0.8,
                 vertex.label.degree = adjusted_angles * pi / 180,
                 vertex.label.cex = 0.7,
                 edge.curved = 0.2,
                 main = "",                              # No title
                 rescale = FALSE,
                 xlim = c(-3.2, 3.2),
                 ylim = c(-3.2, 3.2),
                 asp = 1)
        }

    # Create the circular network plot
        create_subtle_green_plot(optimal_alpha_layout, alphabetical_order)



# ==============================================================================
# NETWORK ANALYSIS STATISTICS EXTRACTION
# ==============================================================================
    # Use objects already created above
        cooccur_matrix <- cooccur_counts
        veech_table <- co_table
        # site_counts already exists from earlier section
    
    # --- CO-OCCURRENCE MATRIX STATISTICS ---
        # Extract upper triangle of matrix (avoid double counting pairs)
            upper_tri_indices <- upper.tri(cooccur_matrix)
            cooccur_values <- cooccur_matrix[upper_tri_indices]
        
        # Remove zero values for meaningful statistics
            nonzero_cooccur <- cooccur_values[cooccur_values > 0]
        
        # Calculate summary statistics
            cooccur_stats <- list(
                total_pairs = length(cooccur_values),
                pairs_with_cooccurrence = length(nonzero_cooccur),
                mean_cooccurrence = round(mean(nonzero_cooccur), 2),
                median_cooccurrence = round(median(nonzero_cooccur), 2),
                min_cooccurrence = min(nonzero_cooccur),
                max_cooccurrence = max(nonzero_cooccur),
                range_cooccurrence = paste(min(nonzero_cooccur), "-", max(nonzero_cooccur))
            )
    
    # --- IDENTIFY TOP CO-OCCURRING SPECIES PAIRS ---
        # Create data frame of all species pairs with their co-occurrence counts
            species_names <- rownames(cooccur_matrix)
            pair_data <- expand.grid(
                species1 = species_names,
                species2 = species_names,
                stringsAsFactors = FALSE
            ) |>
                dplyr::filter(species1 < species2) |>
                dplyr::mutate(
                    cooccur_count = purrr::map2_dbl(species1, species2, ~ {
                        cooccur_matrix[.x, .y]
                    })
                ) |>
                dplyr::arrange(desc(cooccur_count))
        
        # Top co-occurring pairs
            top_pairs <- pair_data |>
                dplyr::slice_head(n = 2) |>
                dplyr::mutate(
                    pair_name = paste(species1, "and", species2),
                    formatted_result = paste0(pair_name, " (n = ", cooccur_count, " sites)")
                )
    
    # --- IDENTIFY LEAST CO-OCCURRING SPECIES ---
        # Calculate total co-occurrences per species (excluding self)
            species_cooccur_totals <- purrr::map_dbl(species_names, ~ {
                species_row <- cooccur_matrix[.x, ]
                species_col <- cooccur_matrix[, .x]
                sum(species_row) + sum(species_col) - cooccur_matrix[.x, .x]
            }) |>
                purrr::set_names(species_names)
        
        # Identify species with lowest co-occurrence
            least_cooccurring <- species_cooccur_totals |>
                sort() |>
                head(3)
    
    # --- VEECH POSITIVE ASSOCIATIONS ---
        # Extract significantly positive associations (p_gt <= 0.05)
            positive_associations <- veech_table |>
                dplyr::filter(p_gt <= 0.05) |>
                dplyr::arrange(p_gt) |>
                dplyr::mutate(
                    species_pair = paste(sp1_name, "and", sp2_name),
                    p_value_formatted = paste0("p = ", round(p_gt, 4))
                )
        
        # Count and format positive associations
            n_positive_pairs <- nrow(positive_associations)
            positive_pairs_text <- if (n_positive_pairs > 0) {
                positive_associations |>
                    dplyr::slice_head(n = 3) |>
                    dplyr::pull(species_pair) |>
                    paste(collapse = ", ")
            } else {
                "No significant positive associations detected"
            }
    
    # --- COMPILE RESULTS SUMMARY ---
        network_analysis_summary <- list(
            cooccurrence_statistics = cooccur_stats,
            top_cooccurring_pairs = top_pairs,
            least_cooccurring_species = least_cooccurring,
            positive_associations = list(
                count = n_positive_pairs,
                pairs = positive_pairs_text,
                full_table = positive_associations
            ),
            species_site_counts = site_counts
        )
    
    # Export to global environment
        assign("network_analysis_summary", network_analysis_summary, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    
    # --- EXPORT COMPLETE ANALYSIS ---
        assign("complete_cooccurrence_analysis", list(
            circular_graph = cooccur_graph,
            circular_layout = optimal_alpha_layout,
            alphabetical_order = alphabetical_order,
            veech_analysis = co,
            veech_table = co_table,
            veech_network = veech_network,
            site_counts = site_counts,
            cooccur_matrix = cooccur_counts
        ), envir = .GlobalEnv)
    
    # --- PRINT KEY RESULTS FOR MANUSCRIPT ---
        cat("=== COMPLETE CO-OCCURRENCE ANALYSIS FINISHED ===\n\n")
        cat("Circular network: Clean plot with no title\n")
        cat("Veech network: Interactive statistical co-occurrence analysis\n\n")
        
        cat("=== NETWORK ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===\n\n")
        cat("Co-occurrence Statistics:\n")
        cat("  Mean co-occurrence:", cooccur_stats$mean_cooccurrence, "sites\n")
        cat("  Median co-occurrence:", cooccur_stats$median_cooccurrence, "sites\n")
        cat("  Range:", cooccur_stats$range_cooccurrence, "sites\n")
        cat("  Total species pairs with co-occurrence:", cooccur_stats$pairs_with_cooccurrence, "\n\n")
        
        cat("Top Co-occurring Species Pairs:\n")
        purrr::walk(top_pairs$formatted_result, ~ cat("  ", .x, "\n"))
        cat("\n")
        
        cat("Least Co-occurring Species:\n")
        purrr::iwalk(least_cooccurring, ~ cat("  ", .y, ": ", .x, " total co-occurrences\n"))
        cat("\n")
        
        cat("Veech Positive Associations:\n")
        cat("  Number of significant positive pairs:", n_positive_pairs, "\n")
        if (n_positive_pairs > 0) {
            cat("  Most significant pairs:", positive_pairs_text, "\n")
        }  
```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 19. NETWORK HEATMAP
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r NETWORK HEATMAP}

  # Assume cooccur_counts is your matrix, and colnames/rownames are cleaned
    mat <- cooccur_counts
  
  # Convert to long format for ggplot2
    cooccur_long <- as.data.frame(mat) %>%
      mutate(Species1 = rownames(mat)) %>%
      pivot_longer(
        cols = -Species1,
        names_to = "Species2",
        values_to = "CoOccurrence"
      )
  
  # Order species alphabetically for axes
    species_order <- sort(unique(cooccur_long$Species1))
    cooccur_long <- cooccur_long %>%
      mutate(
        Species1 = factor(Species1, levels = species_order),
        Species2 = factor(Species2, levels = species_order)
      )
  
  # Plot heatmap
    ggplot(cooccur_long, aes(x = Species2, y = Species1, fill = CoOccurrence)) +
      geom_tile(color = "white") +
      geom_text(aes(label = CoOccurrence), color = "black", size = 3) +
      scale_fill_gradientn(
        colours = colorRampPalette(c("#9cdf8d", "#3541A5"))(100),
        name = "Co-occurrences"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank()
      ) +
      coord_fixed() +
      ggtitle("Species Co-occurrence Heatmap")



```



-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHUNK 20. DISTRIBUTION OVERLAP ANALYSES
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r DISTRIBUTION OVERLAP ANALYSES}

  # Set output directory
    output_dir <- "replace w/ output directory"
  
  # Define the 7 species
    target_species <- c("Demar", "Deret", "Haine", "Hapar", "Hapun", "Ixric", "Rhbur")
  
  # Species code to full name mapping
    species_names <- list(
        "Demar" = "Dermacentor marginatus",
        "Deret" = "Dermacentor reticulatus", 
        "Haine" = "Haemaphysalis inermis",
        "Hapar" = "Haemaphysalis parva",
        "Hapun" = "Haemaphysalis punctata",
        "Ixric" = "Ixodes ricinus",
        "Rhbur" = "Rhipicephalus bursa"
    )

# =============================================================================
# 1. NICHE OVERLAP ANALYSIS USING PREDICTION RASTERS
# =============================================================================

  # Extract prediction rasters for ensemble models
    ensemble_rasters <- map(target_species, function(sp) {
        raster_obj <- successful_ensemble_results[[sp]]$ensemble_predictions
        names(raster_obj) <- sp
        return(raster_obj)
    })
    names(ensemble_rasters) <- target_species

  # Calculate pairwise niche overlap using different metrics
    niche_overlap_results <- map_dfr(target_species, function(sp1) {
        map_dfr(target_species, function(sp2) {
            if (sp1 != sp2) {
                raster1 <- ensemble_rasters[[sp1]]
                raster2 <- ensemble_rasters[[sp2]]
                
                # Calculate different overlap metrics
                warren_i <- nicheOverlap(raster1, raster2, stat = 'I')  # Warren's I
                schoener_d <- nicheOverlap(raster1, raster2, stat = 'D')  # Schoener's D
                
                # Pearson correlation between rasters
                correlation <- cor(values(raster1), values(raster2), use = "complete.obs")
                
                # Rank correlation
                rank_corr <- cor(values(raster1), values(raster2), 
                               method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
                
                tibble(
                    Species1 = sp1,
                    Species2 = sp2,
                    Species1_Full = species_names[[sp1]],
                    Species2_Full = species_names[[sp2]],
                    Warrens_I = round(warren_i, 3),
                    Schoeners_D = round(schoener_d, 3),
                    Pearson_Correlation = round(correlation, 3),
                    Spearman_Correlation = round(rank_corr, 3)
                )
            }
        }) %>%
            filter(!is.null(.))
    })

  # Create symmetric matrices for visualization
    create_overlap_matrix <- function(overlap_data, metric) {
        overlap_matrix <- matrix(NA, 
                               nrow = length(target_species), 
                               ncol = length(target_species),
                               dimnames = list(target_species, target_species))
        
        for (i in 1:nrow(overlap_data)) {
            sp1 <- overlap_data$Species1[i]
            sp2 <- overlap_data$Species2[i]
            value <- overlap_data[[metric]][i]
            overlap_matrix[sp1, sp2] <- value
            overlap_matrix[sp2, sp1] <- value  # Make symmetric
        }
        
        # Fill diagonal with 1s
        diag(overlap_matrix) <- 1.0
        
        return(overlap_matrix)
    }

  # Create matrices for each metric
    warrens_matrix <- create_overlap_matrix(niche_overlap_results, "Warrens_I")
    schoeners_matrix <- create_overlap_matrix(niche_overlap_results, "Schoeners_D")
    pearson_matrix <- create_overlap_matrix(niche_overlap_results, "Pearson_Correlation")
    spearman_matrix <- create_overlap_matrix(niche_overlap_results, "Spearman_Correlation")

  # Visualize niche overlap matrices
    png(file.path(output_dir, "niche_overlap_matrices.png"), 
        width = 16, height = 12, units = "in", res = 300)
    
    par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 2))
  
  # Warren's I
    corrplot(warrens_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper", 
             title = "Warren's I Niche Overlap", mar = c(0,0,2,0),
             col = colorRampPalette(c("white", "lightblue", "darkblue"))(100),
             addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = 0.8, tl.cex = 0.8)
  
  # Schoener's D
    corrplot(schoeners_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper",
             title = "Schoener's D Niche Overlap", mar = c(0,0,2,0),
             col = colorRampPalette(c("white", "lightgreen", "darkgreen"))(100),
             addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = 0.8, tl.cex = 0.8)
  
  # Pearson correlation
    corrplot(pearson_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper",
             title = "Pearson Correlation", mar = c(0,0,2,0),
             col = colorRampPalette(c("red", "white", "blue"))(100),
             addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = 0.8, tl.cex = 0.8)
  
  # Spearman correlation
    corrplot(spearman_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper",
             title = "Spearman Correlation", mar = c(0,0,2,0),
             col = colorRampPalette(c("red", "white", "blue"))(100),
             addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = 0.8, tl.cex = 0.8)
    
    dev.off()

# =============================================================================
# 2. VARIABLE IMPORTANCE COMPARISON
# =============================================================================

  # Extract variable importance for all species
    importance_comparison <- map_dfr(target_species, function(sp) {
        importance_data <- successful_ensemble_results[[sp]]$ensemble_importance
        importance_data %>%
            mutate(species = sp) %>%
            dplyr::select(species, var, rel.inf, frequency)
    })
  
  # Create importance matrix
    importance_wide <- importance_comparison %>%
        dplyr::select(species, var, rel.inf) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = species, values_from = rel.inf, values_fill = 0) %>%
        column_to_rownames("var")
  
  # Create heatmap of variable importance
    importance_heatmap <- importance_wide %>%
        rownames_to_column("Variable") %>%
        pivot_longer(-Variable, names_to = "Species", values_to = "Importance") %>%
        ggplot(aes(x = Species, y = Variable, fill = Importance)) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Relative\nImportance (%)") +
        labs(
            title = "Variable Importance Comparison Across Species",
            x = "Species", y = "Environmental Variables"
        ) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(
            axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
            axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8)
        )
  
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "variable_importance_heatmap.png"),
         importance_heatmap, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)

# =============================================================================
# 3. PREDICTION CORRELATION ANALYSIS
# =============================================================================

  # Extract values from all prediction rasters
    prediction_values <- map_dfc(target_species, function(sp) {
        raster_obj <- successful_ensemble_results[[sp]]$ensemble_predictions
        values_vec <- values(raster_obj)
        tibble(!!sp := values_vec)
    }) %>%
        na.omit()
  
  # Calculate correlation matrix
    pred_cor_matrix <- cor(prediction_values, method = "pearson")
  
  # Create correlation plot
    png(file.path(output_dir, "prediction_correlations.png"), 
        width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
    
    corrplot(pred_cor_matrix, 
             method = "color", 
             type = "upper",
             order = "hclust",
             col = colorRampPalette(c("red", "white", "blue"))(100),
             addCoef.col = "black",
             number.cex = 0.8,
             tl.cex = 0.8,
             title = "Prediction Correlations Between Species",
             mar = c(0,0,2,0))
    
    dev.off()

# =============================================================================
# 4. SUMMARY RESULTS TABLES
# =============================================================================

  # Create comprehensive comparison table with averages
    comparison_summary <- tibble(
        Species_Code = target_species,
        Species_Name = map_chr(target_species, ~ species_names[[.x]]),
        Mean_Warrens_I = map_dbl(target_species, function(sp) {
            mean(niche_overlap_results$Warrens_I[
                niche_overlap_results$Species1 == sp | 
                niche_overlap_results$Species2 == sp], na.rm = TRUE)
        }),
        Mean_Schoeners_D = map_dbl(target_species, function(sp) {
            mean(niche_overlap_results$Schoeners_D[
                niche_overlap_results$Species1 == sp | 
                niche_overlap_results$Species2 == sp], na.rm = TRUE)
        }),
        Mean_Pearson_Correlation = map_dbl(target_species, function(sp) {
            mean(niche_overlap_results$Pearson_Correlation[
                niche_overlap_results$Species1 == sp | 
                niche_overlap_results$Species2 == sp], na.rm = TRUE)
        }),
        Mean_Spearman_Correlation = map_dbl(target_species, function(sp) {
            mean(niche_overlap_results$Spearman_Correlation[
                niche_overlap_results$Species1 == sp | 
                niche_overlap_results$Species2 == sp], na.rm = TRUE)
        })
    ) %>%
        mutate(
            Mean_Warrens_I = round(Mean_Warrens_I, 3),
            Mean_Schoeners_D = round(Mean_Schoeners_D, 3),
            Mean_Pearson_Correlation = round(Mean_Pearson_Correlation, 3),
            Mean_Spearman_Correlation = round(Mean_Spearman_Correlation, 3)
        )

  # Create complete pairwise comparison table
    pairwise_complete <- niche_overlap_results %>%
        arrange(desc(Warrens_I)) %>%
        mutate(
            Species_Pair = paste(Species1_Full, "vs", Species2_Full)
        ) %>%
        dplyr::select(
            Species_Pair,
            Species1,
            Species2,
            Warrens_I,
            Schoeners_D,
            Pearson_Correlation,
            Spearman_Correlation
        )

  # Save all results
    write.csv(pairwise_complete, 
              file.path(output_dir, "niche_overlap_all_pairs.csv"), 
              row.names = FALSE)
    
    write.csv(comparison_summary, 
              file.path(output_dir, "species_overlap_summary.csv"), 
              row.names = FALSE)
    
    write.csv(importance_comparison, 
              file.path(output_dir, "variable_importance_comparison.csv"), 
              row.names = FALSE)

  # Print summary results
    print(comparison_summary)
    print(pairwise_complete)
  
  # Quick summary statistics
    cat("Highest niche overlap (Warren's I):", 
        pairwise_complete$Species_Pair[1], "=", pairwise_complete$Warrens_I[1], "\n")
    cat("Lowest niche overlap (Warren's I):", 
        pairwise_complete$Species_Pair[nrow(pairwise_complete)], "=", 
        pairwise_complete$Warrens_I[nrow(pairwise_complete)], "\n")
    cat("Mean Warren's I across all pairs:", 
        round(mean(pairwise_complete$Warrens_I), 3), "\n")

  # Set output directory for figures
    output_dir <- "OUTPUT DIRECTORY"
    dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  # Prepare complete matrix data (including diagonal)
    matrix_data <- map_dfr(target_species, function(sp1) {
        map_dfr(target_species, function(sp2) {
            if (sp1 == sp2) {
                # Diagonal - perfect overlap
                  tibble(
                      Species1 = sp1,
                      Species2 = sp2,
                      Warrens_I = 1.0,
                      Schoeners_D = 1.0,
                      Pearson_Correlation = 1.0,
                      Spearman_Correlation = 1.0
                  )
            } else {
                # Find the corresponding pair in niche_overlap_results
                  pair_data <- niche_overlap_results %>%
                      filter((Species1 == sp1 & Species2 == sp2) | 
                             (Species1 == sp2 & Species2 == sp1))
                
                if (nrow(pair_data) > 0) {
                    tibble(
                        Species1 = sp1,
                        Species2 = sp2,
                        Warrens_I = pair_data$Warrens_I[1],
                        Schoeners_D = pair_data$Schoeners_D[1],
                        Pearson_Correlation = pair_data$Pearson_Correlation[1],
                        Spearman_Correlation = pair_data$Spearman_Correlation[1]
                    )
                }
            }
        })
    }) %>%
        filter(!is.na(Warrens_I))

  # Function to create matrix plot with values
    create_matrix_plot <- function(data, metric_col, title, colors) {
        ggplot(data, aes(x = Species1, y = Species2, fill = !!sym(metric_col))) +
            geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
            geom_text(aes(label = round(!!sym(metric_col), 3)), 
                     color = "white", size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
            scale_fill_gradientn(
                colors = colors,
                name = "",
                limits = c(0, 1),
                breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0)
            ) +
            labs(title = title) +
            theme_minimal() +
            theme(
                axis.title = element_blank(),
                axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
                axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
                panel.grid = element_blank(),
                plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
                legend.position = "right",
                legend.key.height = unit(1, "cm"),
                legend.key.width = unit(0.5, "cm"),
                panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
                plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA)
            ) +
            coord_fixed()
    }

  # Define color schemes (all blue-green variations, light to dark)
    warrens_colors <- c("#E3F3FF", "#C5E3FF", "#A7D3FF", "#89C2FF", "#6BB2FF", "#4C9FFF", "#2378D4", "#13539A", "#103D66", "#10243D")
    schoeners_colors <- c("#EAE6F8", "#C8C4EB", "#A7A3DF", "#8883D3", "#6C66C7", "#584FB0", "#463C96", "#372D7A", "#28205C", "#18133E")
    pearson_colors <- c("#E7FFF3", "#C4F9E3", "#A0F4D2", "#7DEFC2", "#59E8B0", "#36E19E", "#24B37B", "#187D59", "#145C43", "#0A3325")
    spearman_colors <- c("#F6FFE3", "#E7FFB9", "#D7FF8B", "#C8FF5E", "#B6EA35", "#A3D126", "#84A91C", "#668214", "#475C0C", "#293605")
  
  # Create the four matrices
    warrens_plot <- create_matrix_plot(matrix_data, "Warrens_I", 
                                       "Warren's I", warrens_colors)
    
    schoeners_plot <- create_matrix_plot(matrix_data, "Schoeners_D", 
                                     "Schoener's D", schoeners_colors)
    
    pearson_plot <- create_matrix_plot(matrix_data, "Pearson_Correlation", 
                                     "Pearson Correlation", pearson_colors)
    
    spearman_plot <- create_matrix_plot(matrix_data, "Spearman_Correlation", 
                                      "Spearman Correlation", spearman_colors)
```
